description: Brimming with character and thoughtfully renovated by the current owner, this charming two-bedroom Victorian cottage offers 628 sq ft of bright, well-balanced living space. The open-plan reception, kitchen and dining area has been extended to create a wonderfully light and sociable heart to the home, perfect for both quiet evenings and easy entertaining. Upstairs, you’ll find two comfortable bedrooms and a modern bathroom, while outside, a private garden and dedicated driveway add rare convenience to this kind of period property. Set within one of Forest Gate's finest streets in the Woodgrange Conservation area, the house sits just a short 10-minute stroll from the wide-open greenery of Wanstead Flats. Forest Gate and Manor Park Stations (Elizabeth Line) and Wanstead Park (Overground) are both within easy reach, too.IF YOU LIVED HERE...There’s no need to circle the block for parking when you’ve got your own dedicated driveway, a rare luxury that makes hauling heavy shopping bags blissfully easy. A passion flower climbs along the boundary fence and over the porch, offering a warm, quietly charming welcome home.Step inside to a bright, carefully curated open-plan living space where pale walls and reclaimed chevron parquet flooring run seamlessly throughout. At the front, generous windows dressed with plantation shutters fill the room with light, showing off details like the ceiling rose and a cast-iron fireplace framed by Carrera marble. A step down leads you naturally into the dining area, softly lit by twin pendant lights. The kitchen extends down both sides, finished with immaculate ice-blue cabinetry and brushed brass fittings. A skylight set into the pitched roof floods the space with daylight, while French doors open onto the garden, blurring the line between inside and out.Outside, the garden stretches over 20 feet, beginning with a paved patio and built-in cedar wood seating. A gravel path runs through a neat lawn to a bespoke high-quality redwood timber garden room framed by mature apple and cherry trees, providing annual fruit and spring blossom. Jasmine, lavender, foxgloves, clematis and many bulbs provide colour in spring to autumn, creating a private, leafy retreat to enjoy all year round.You’ll appreciate the purpose-built under-stair storage for shoes, coats and bags as you ascend the wrought iron staircase to the first floor. There, you’ll find two double bedrooms and a bathroom that make clever use of space. The bathroom keeps things crisp and uncomplicated, featuring a white suite, bath-shower combo with black trim, full-height vintage tiling, and Fired Earth floor tiling that adds a refined touch.Both bedrooms are comfortable doubles. The main spans the full width of the house, complete with bespoke built-in cupboards, a dressing table or desk and twin sash windows overlooking the tree-lined street. Facing south, it’s bathed in light from morning through to dusk. The rear bedroom looks out over the garden and shares the same sense of calm and attention to detail.WHAT ELSE?Wanstead Park Station (7 mins) connects to the Victoria Line via Blackhorse Road for easy West End access, while nearby Forest Gate (15 mins) offers fast Elizabeth Line links to central London, Canary Wharf, and Heathrow. You’re truly spoilt for choice for a new local - The Golden Fleece, Pretty Decent Beer Co, Cider House E7, and The Holly Tree are all within easy reach.The wide open spaces of Wanstead Flats and the start of Epping Forest are just moments away, ideal for morning jogs, lazy dog walks, or letting the kids burn off energy at the nearby playground.Kitchen/Dining/Living Room - 3.68 x 9.42 (12'0" x 30'10") - Bedroom 1 - 3.61 x 2.57 (11'10" x 8'5") - Bedroom 2 - 3.64 x 2.06 (11'11" x 6'9") - Bathroom - 1.73 x 1.54 (5'8" x 5'0") - Garden - 3.9 x 12.5 (12'9" x 41'0") - A WORD FROM THE OWNER..."When we decided to move to Forest Gate a few years ago, we were drawn by the lovely characterful houses full of history, the wide leafy streets and the proximity to the large green spaces of Wanstead Flats & Epping Forest. We’ve made good use of the nearby parks for running, Sunday strolls, feeding the ducks or summer picnics. Equally we’ve loved the independent cafes and restaurants that have popped up since we’ve been here, Giovanna’s, Ramble, Wild Goose, The Holly Tree, Yellow Trike, to name a few.We’ve enjoyed renovating this house and bringing back the old Victorian charm. The open plan downstairs makes it bright and spacious. There’s plenty of hidden storage tucked away too. The garden has been a labour of love and in the spring and summer months it’s a tranquil oasis of colour with butterflies, bees, squirrels and birds.The neighbourhood has a lovely community with friendly faces always happy to say ‘good morning’. Quick links into town mean that for work, the Elizabeth Line gets us to Stratford in 5mins and Liverpool Street in 12 minutes so both super easy commutes.It’s been the perfect home to enjoy as a couple and then start our family. We’ve made wonderful memories here and I’m sure the new owners will too."BrochuresWindsor Road, Forest GateProperty Material InformationAML InformationBrochure

location: Villa Fontaine, Sydenham Hill, London, SE26
features: Large open plan reception, kitchen, dining room
Niche, boutique development of only 9 apartments
Two bedroom, two bathroom (one with ensuite)
Juliette balcony & large communal roof terrace
description: Designed by Craftworks, a global award winning company and one of Britain's most enterprising and intelligent architect practices, voted one of the world's top 50 best-emerging architects by Wallpaper magazine. The overall appearance of the building is calm, balanced and robust, with a subtle visual richness. The elevation is contemporary in style, but is tied in to its context by the vertical proportions of its bay windows and the use of world renowned Peterson hand made Danish brick and tile, there is also bespoke joinery throughout. The exterior has been designed with considered attention to quality and detail of the materials selected while adhering to the local context and character. Defined by its craftsmanship and practicality, there are far reaching views from each unit, with a double aspect layout with views from front to back. Each apartment has large living areas with bay window concept with full height windows allowing ample daylight and views out. The rooms are well designed and purposeful layouts that are both functional and harmonious. There are two generous double bedrooms towards the rear of the plan, one with an en suite. A family bathroom in the centre sits between the living and sleeping spaces. Features includes ample storage throughout, background ventilation to all spaces, in built desks in bedrooms, underfloor heating, hand made solid wood kitchens, solid wood flooring throughout, unique design of solid wood wall and ceiling panelling throughout, solid Corian style kitchen worktops, full height solid wood doors, high security with intercom and secure by design layouts, secure cycle storage with green roof tops. The Area Villa Fontaine sits beside Dulwich and Sydenham Woods, both wildlife nature reserves. This, combined with Dulwich and Sydenham Golf Course, as well as an allotment and sports club ground, provides unbroken green space as far as Dulwich Park and Lordship Lane. The lake, maze, and open spaces of Crystal Palace Park are also a short walk away, as are the beautiful expanses of Sydenham Wells Park and Dulwich Park. Other local attractions include The Dulwich Picture Gallery, The Horniman Museum and the local town centres of Crystal Palace and Dulwich Village. Crystal Palace, which has previously been named number five on Lonely Planets "greatest little-known neighbourhoods in the world", is a 15-minute walk away and is a vibrant area with award-winning restaurants and bars as well as having a thriving arts and music scene. Church Road winds through Crystal Palace, surrounded by cafes and restaurants, including Brown & Green, Four Boroughs, Palazzo, and the Affinity Brew Shop. We've written more about our recommendations in the area in our Journal. Dulwich Village is also a 15-minute walk from the house, and the street is well-placed for Ofsted-rated "Outstanding;" primary and secondary state schools, as well as some of the best private schools in London. There is a short walk on a footpath to Sydenham Hill Station, which runs services to London Victoria (13 minutes), Blackfriars (20 minutes) and City Thameslink/Farringdon (22 minutes). Connecting trains (via Sydenham) will take you to Canada Water in 20 minutes and Shoreditch High Street in 30 minutes. features: Walking distance to Brixton and Clapham High Streets
description: Keating Estates are proud to present to market an exquisite two double bedroom split-level maisonette with a balcony and private front door, set within a handsome Victorian terraced property in a popular, quiet residential pocket between Brixton and Clapham.Full Description - Keating Estates are proud to present this beautifully presented two-bedroom split-level apartment with a private balcony, set within a charming Victorian maisonette with its own private entrance. Arranged over the first and second floors of an attractive period property and boasting over 1,000 square feet of internal space, this home perfectly balances contemporary design with classic Victorian character. Kingswood Road is a neighbourly street, with an annual street party, positioned close to the action of both Brixton and Clapham, yet far enough away to retain a sense of calm - enhanced by the recent Low Traffic Neighbourhood - with a community-run pub and multiple shops just moments away at the top of the road.Renovated and modernised between 2022 and 2023, this well-maintained home showcases a stylish blend of contemporary and period features, finished to a high specification throughout. Attention has been given to both style and practicality, including original or newly laid hardwood flooring running through most of the home, complemented by plush new carpeting where softness underfoot is most desirable.As you enter the property from the ground floor, a private internal staircase leads you up to the first floor, where the sense of space becomes immediately apparent. The split-level design ensures a sense of privacy and separation between the principal bedroom and social spaces below, which is particularly appealing for families, those working from home, or anyone sharing with others.Forming the heart of the home, a bright and airy reception room is located at the front of the property, with three large windows flooding the room with natural light and enhancing the sense of openness. Bursting with character, the reception room retains beautiful cornicing, a picture rail, and an elegant ceiling rose, with new hardwood flooring adding warmth. A feature fireplace forms the focal point of the room, framed by bespoke shelving and cabinetry built into the alcoves, offering both style and practical storage. This elegant space offers plenty of room for both lounging and entertaining, making it a natural hub of the home.Moving through to the rear of the first floor, the dine-in kitchen combines modern style with everyday practicality. Sleek cream cabinetry is paired with warm wooden worktops and a soft green tiled splashback, creating a fresh yet welcoming feel. Integrated appliances, a butler sink, and ample storage make the space highly functional, a lovely place to cook and entertain, enhanced further by large windows with leafy green views.Natural light floods in from the balcony door, creating a bright and airy atmosphere for cooking and hosting. The balcony itself extends the living space outdoors, offering a private and peaceful spot to enjoy morning coffee, relax with a book, or al fresco dinners.Leading off the kitchen is a separate W/C, thoughtfully positioned for visiting guests. Opposite, the smart, neutrally tiled family bathroom features a walk-in shower and a wash basin with vanity storage. The space is well-ventilated and attractively finished with modern fixtures and fittings, including a heated towel rail.Both bedrooms are spacious doubles, with the bedroom on this first floor benefiting from a peaceful outlook and good levels of natural light. A decorative fireplace is the focal point of the room, with built-in storage too. This is a versatile space that can be repurposed as a guest room, home office, or nursery if both bedrooms are not required.The upper floor is dedicated to a luxurious principal suite. This expansive bedroom has been designed with comfort in mind. Built-in storage keeps the room clutter-free, while the en-suite bathroom exudes sophistication, complete with striking blue tiles, a deep bathtub, and a separate shower. The space is enhanced by a large Velux window, which floods the room with natural light throughout the day.The location is fantastic, Kingswood Road is a popular street forming part of the Low Traffic Neighbourhood and prides itself on its real sense of community, with residents enjoying annual street parties and a welcoming, neighbourly atmosphere, with Richard Atkins School at the top of the road.On the doorstep there are two large-brand supermarkets, off-licences and a gym. The property is a twenty-minute walk or swift bus ride to both Brixton and Clapham and all that these two in-demand areas have to offer. Situated a similar distance from both locations, potential purchasers will take advantage of some of the best transport links for a speedy route into central London, with the options of first stop on the Victoria Line, the Northern Line, the Overground, trains and multiples buses that run up and down both Kings Avenue and Brixton Hill. Potential purchasers will benefit from fantastic local amenities, a few examples include the green open spaces of Brockwell Park with its 1930s Lido and Clapham Common, and all that Brixton Village, POP Brixton, Clapham High Street and Clapham Old Town have to offer in terms of food and festivities. Abbeville Village is a short stroll through the quiet residential streets, just the other side of Kings Avenue, and the Venn Street Market by Clapham Common tube is the perfect place to whittle down a few hours on a Saturday afternoon.Share of freehold.Chain-free.BrochuresKingswood Road, SW2Brochure

features: FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM AT E10HOMES_LEYTON
description: A bright and spacious, three bedroom family home. With a ground floor WC, first floor family bathroom and a private rear garden. There's lots of scope for development here, and Lloyd Park, Waltham Forest Feel Good Centre and Wood Street are all within easy reach.With three generous bedrooms, a first floor bathroom and well arranged ground floor living, this handsome family home also has lots of potential for further development, perhaps even a loft extension, subject to the usual planning permissions.You're well connected by road and nearby overground services to the rest of London, but there are also plenty of parks, pubs and vibrant shopping areas within easy walking distance. Lloyd Park in particular is just a third of a mile on foot, and you also have your own private garden.YOUR NEW HOMEStep inside and you'll find a bright, 140 square foot reception room on your right. In here, daylight pours in through the modernised, UPVC bay window and smooth, engineered hardwood runs underfoot.Next door, the hardwood flooring continues in your 125 square foot dining room. Another light, naturally bright space, looking out to the rear of your home.A connecting doorway opens into your stylish, 100 square foot kitchen, where you'll find ranks of sleek white cabinets, solid timber worktops, glossy white splashback tilework, an integrated oven with gas hob and extractor hood, and a dual aspect with garden views.From here, you've access to a smart, convenient ground floor WC and another doorway leads out to your private rear garden. Surrounded by solid timber fencing and with a spacious stretch of lawn and paving, this tranquil space has lots of potential for outdoor entertaining.Back inside, and heading up to the first floor, you'll find your 150 square foot, principal bedroom to the front. Another bright space with more of that smooth hardwood flooring and a twin set of frontal aspect windows.Your second double bedroom can be found next door, similarly decorated in a tasteful neutral palette and overlooking the gardenContinuing along the landing, you'll arrive in your family bathroom. A clean, white space with metro tilework and a modern suite that includes a chrome mixer shower over the bathtub.Finally, your third bedroom is located at the end of the landing, overlooking the garden and with the same gentle colour scheme as your first and second bedrooms.YOUR NEW NEIGHBOURHOODWood Street station is only a twenty minute walk away, where you can catch the newly christened Weaver Overground line and ride straight through to Liverpool Street in twenty two minutes. Driving around London also couldn't be easier, with the North Circular motorway only five minutes from your front door. Also just five minutes away on foot, The Dog and Duck could be your new local. This family friendly gastropub has a lovely walled beer garden and offers a delicious range of sourdough pizzas.You have two bustling, vibrant streets within an easy fifteen minute walk, Wood Street and Hoe Street, both lined with great places to eat and boutique shops and Wood Street offers the much loved indoor market, bursting with vintage finds.One of Hoe Street's most popular venues is Ye Olde Rose and Crown, where you can enjoy the traditional pub atmosphere and take in some culture at the same time, in their cosy, well programmed, upstairs theatre.There are two fantastic coffee shops within a five minute walk of your new home, The Galley and Ruttle & Rowe. Both extremely popular with local residents and wonderful places for a weekend catch up with friends over a speciality coffee. For exercise and pampering, the Waltham Forest Feel Good Centre is only ten minutes away on foot. This multi milion pound leisure development has a great range of facilities including a state of the art, multistation gym and a luxury spa. There are two beautiful, open, green spaces for you to explore nearby. Lloyd Park is just ten minutes away, with immaculately cultivated gardens and the historic William Morris Gallery. For cycling and walking, Epping Forest is less than a mile from your home, for you to explore to your heart's content. The younger members of your family will have a number of local schools to choose from. There are twenty four primary and secondary schools within a mile of your home and the closest schools, Chapel End and Sir George Monoux College, provide education services from infants through to post sixteen education.EPC Rating: D

This spacious two-bedroom apartment is set on the fourth floor of Baltimore House within the sought-after Riverside West development. Presented in excellent condition throughout, the property offers two well-proportioned double bedrooms, both with fitted storage, and two bathrooms, including a principal en suite. The bright reception room opens onto a private balcony with attractive river views, while a contemporary kitchen, separated from the living space by sleek pocket doors, adds both style and practicality. Residents of Riverside West enjoy a range of amenities, including secure underground parking, a 24-hour concierge service, and access to a private residents' gym. The property is offered with no onward chain and benefits from a long lease of 999 years from 1 November 2004. Location Baltimore House is situated within the highly desirable Battersea Reach development on Juniper Drive, nestled on the banks of the River Thames in SW18. This riverside address affords a rare combination of peaceful waterside ambiance and excellent connectivity to central London. Wandsworth Town station is within easy walking distance, providing frequent rail services to Waterloo (approx. 10-15 minutes) and convenient onward connections to the Victoria line at Vauxhall. Clapham Junction is also close by, offering extensive rail links across the capital, while river transport options and local bus routes serve Battersea and Wandsworth with ease. Road access is strong, with the A3/M3 corridor readily accessible and nearby river crossings such as Wandsworth Bridge connecting to areas north of the Thames. As part of the Battersea Reach development, residents benefit from 24-hour concierge, on-site gym and fitness facilities, and secure underground parking. Everyday conveniences are close at hand, including a Tesco Express, riverside cafes, restaurants and bars, plus local shops and services. The nearby Old York Road conservation area offers a charming high street feel with independent cafés and boutiques, while the Thames Path provides attractive riverside walks both east toward central London and west into Battersea and beyond.

Spacious and filled with natural light from large windows, the apartments are designed to feel open and inviting while offering the flexibility modern lifestyles demand. Open-plan kitchen/reception areas create the perfect setting for socialising, while quieter, more private spaces ensure comfort for work or rest. Each apartment features sleek, modern kitchens with high-quality appliances, energy-efficient designs to reduce environmental impact, and private outdoor spaces such as balconies to enjoy the views. The interiors are an embodiment of refined design, inspired by modernist urban aesthetics in the communal areas, while the apartments themselves continue the premium theme. Cricklewood is an excellent choice for families, offering a combination of top-rated schools, green spaces, and a variety of housing options, including larger homes ideal for growing families. features: Newly Fitted Bathroom & Ground Floor WC
Short Walk To George Lane & Station
description: This handsome three-bedroom Victorian terrace blends period character with thoughtful updates and a great sense of space. The brick façade and driveway with EV charger make a strong first impression, while inside, original features sit comfortably alongside modern touches. A mature rear garden extends the living space outdoors, complemented by a convenient ground floor WC and a newly fitted bathroom. Perfectly positioned opposite Epping Forest and a short stroll from George Lane and South Woodford Station, it offers both comfort and connectivity.IF YOU LIVED HERE…A classic brick-fronted home of timeless character, this property pairs elegant period detailing with a welcoming modern flow. Its frontage, with a tall bay window and warm brickwork, exudes balance and charm, while the smart black door and paved driveway create a neat and practical approach.Inside, the hallway sets a warm tone, leading into a generous reception room where natural daylight floods through the bay window. A period fireplace forms a striking centrepiece, framed by bespoke shelving, and polished wooden floors bring warmth and texture underfoot. It’s a beautifully proportioned space, ideal for relaxing or entertaining.To the rear, the open-plan kitchen and dining area feels bright and inviting, with soft tones and timber flooring creating a sense of continuity. Glazed doors open into a brightly lit walkway with patterned tiling and a partially glazed roof, leading to the garden and a neatly positioned WC.Outside, the garden stretches generously from the house, combining a broad paved terrace with a deep lawn framed by mature shrubs and planting. It’s an appealing outdoor setting, perfect for gatherings, play, or quiet afternoons surrounded by greenery.Upstairs, two bedrooms offer calm and comfort, the first featuring twin windows, built-in wardrobes and a period fireplace, while the second enjoys garden views and a relaxed, versatile layout. The bathroom includes both a bath and separate shower, finished in dark textured tiles with elegant brass accents.The top floor presents a spacious principal bedroom, filled with light from two skylights and dual windows, fitted wardrobes and useful eaves storage, completing this serene retreat.The location offers an appealing mix of leafy surroundings, independent shops and welcoming local spots. Just minutes away, Epping Forest stretches for miles, offering woodland walks and peaceful green escapes, while Churchfields Park brings a friendly community feel with its playground, open lawns and tennis courts. A short stroll leads to George Lane, home to boutiques, cafés and restaurants such as Bobo & Wild and The George, along with a local cinema and a great choice of everyday amenities. For dining out, Jones & Sons serves excellent modern British dishes nearby, and Churchfields Infant and Junior School, around a ten-minute walk away, is rated Outstanding.WHAT ELSE?South Woodford Station is just over a fifteen-minute walk away, offering Central Line connections for swift journeys into the City and West End. The area is also well served by local bus routes, providing easy links to neighbouring spots such as Wanstead and Leytonstone. Whether commuting or exploring nearby areas, travel from here is straightforward and well connected in every direction.Reception Room - 3.26 x 3.79 (10'8" x 12'5") - Kitchen / Diner - 4.25 x 6.16 (13'11" x 20'2") - Wc - Bedroom - 4.19 x 3.27 (13'8" x 10'8") - Bedroom - 3.34 x 2.34 (10'11" x 7'8") - Bathroom - 2.19 x 2.68 (7'2" x 8'9") - Bedroom - 3.65 x 6.01 (11'11" x 19'8") - Garden - 4.3 x 13.8 (14'1" x 45'3") - A WORD FROM THE OWNER..."We’ve absolutely loved calling this house our home. Nestled on a lovely, friendly road just a stone’s throw from Epping Forest, it has always offered the perfect balance between peaceful surroundings and great connections. The neighbours here are truly wonderful, there’s a real sense of community, the kind where everyone looks out for one another. The home itself has been carefully maintained over the years, full of character and charm with beautiful period features throughout. The three bedrooms are all a generous size, and the location couldn’t be better, close to the Waterworks roundabout, with excellent transport links making it easy to reach both the city and surrounding green spaces. It’s a home that has been loved and looked after, and we hope the next owners will enjoy it just as much as we have."BrochuresPeel Road, South WoodfordProperty Material InformationAML InformationBrochure

features: Short Walk to Forest Gate Station
description: This Victorian terrace combines generous space with a fresh, modern feel, offering three double bedrooms and two well-finished bathrooms across two floors. The interiors are bright and contemporary, designed for comfortable everyday living with a sense of openness throughout. A landscaped south-west facing garden provides an inviting outdoor escape, while the large garden studio adds valuable flexibility for work or leisure. With its refined finish, chain-free status and easy access to Forest Gate Station, this home offers a well-balanced blend of style, practicality and convenience in a sought-after location.IF YOU LIVED HERE…The property’s dark-painted façade gives it a distinctive edge, complemented by matching window frames and a streamlined front door. Subtle detailing enhances its period character, while the overall design feels bold yet inviting, setting the tone for the home within.Inside, a hallway with understairs storage leads into an impressive open-plan reception room. This generous space flows naturally from front to back, filled with light and framed by wide-plank flooring and pale walls. French doors open directly onto the garden, blurring the boundary between indoors and out. The adjoining kitchen continues the airy, contemporary feel, with crisp white finishes, twin windows and a glazed door offering easy access to the outside space. Beyond, the bathroom pairs soft white tiling with contrasting fixtures and a full-sized bath, creating a calm and elegant atmosphere.Mature trees frame the outdoor space beautifully, offering privacy and a sense of seclusion. The paved terrace invites relaxed dining, while the raised lawn draws the eye towards the studio at the far end. Surrounded by greenery, this space feels both open and sheltered, a peaceful retreat that balances nature and city living. The studio itself is bright and versatile, ideal as a workspace, creative hub or simply a quiet place to unwind.Moving upstairs, the landing includes built-in storage and leads to three inviting bedrooms. Each features fitted storage, soft tones and plenty of light, with the main room offering generous proportions. The shower room completes the layout, finished in stone-effect tiling for a refined, understated look.The surrounding area offers a vibrant mix of independent cafés, green spaces and family-friendly spots. Just a short stroll away, the Forest Gate Railway Arches host local favourites such as Ramble Café, Joyau and Wanstead Kitchen, a lively stretch lined with artisan coffee, global flavours and a warm neighbourhood energy. The Holly Tree pub adds to the community feel, offering a welcoming space for families and friends alike. Forest Lane Park, with its playground and open lawns, provides a peaceful pocket of nature close to home, while the vast expanse of Wanstead Flats invites long walks and outdoor adventures. Excellent local schools, including the outstanding Earlham Primary, make this a particularly appealing area for families.WHAT ELSE?Around five minutes from your door, Forest Gate Station offers fast and frequent Elizabeth line services into the City, West End and beyond. Its excellent connectivity makes daily travel effortless and reliable. Wanstead Park Station is also within easy reach, providing Overground links across East London. Together, they make commuting and weekend exploring wonderfully convenient, keeping you closely connected to the best of the capital.Reception Room - 3.42 x 7.84 (11'2" x 25'8" ) - Kitchen - 2.35 x 3.59 (7'8" x 11'9") - Bathroom - 2.17 x 1.68 (7'1" x 5'6") - Bedroom - 4.45 x 3.42 (14'7" x 11'2") - Bedroom - 2.87 x 3.48 (9'4" x 11'5") - Bedroom - 2.44 x 3.91 (8'0" x 12'9" ) - Shower Room - 0.76 x 2.43 (2'5" x 7'11") - Garden Studio - 3.19 x 4.26 (10'5" x 13'11") - Garden - 4.7 x 11.7 (15'5" x 38'4") - A WORD FROM THE EXPERT...Forest Gate is one of London’s best-kept secrets. With its great housing stock, the wide-open green spaces of Wanstead Flats, and a friendly, up-and-coming vibe shaped by independent boutiques, eateries, and bars, it has all the ingredients of a truly special neighbourhood.Weekends here are easily filled with morning coffee and pastries at The Wild Goose Bakery, fresh pasta from Fiore Truck, or a cosy dinner at Giovanna’s (or a fantastic curry from The Wanstead Kitchen). Pair that with a walk across Wanstead Flats or through Wanstead Park, and you have the perfect local day out.Thanks to the Elizabeth line, Forest Gate is brilliantly connected. Liverpool Street is just 12 minutes away, Canary Wharf around 15, and Heathrow can be reached directly in under an hour. It is this mix of character, community and convenience that makes the area so appealing.Above all, Forest Gate has an arty, independent feel and a strong sense of community that the locals actively nurture and support, giving the area its unique charm. This stunning one-bedroom flat is arranged over the ground floor of an attractive Victorian house, situated on a popular residential road close to the open spaces of Clapham Common. Measuring approximately 750 sq, the property offers well-proportioned principal rooms and a bright, airy feel throughout. A huge draw to this fantastic flat is the patioed garden which is ideal for alfresco dining and entertaining during the summer months. The spacious and bright reception room features a beautiful feature fireplace with bespoke shelving flanking the chimney breast, intricate cornicing, and a large bay window that floods the room with natural light. Adjacent to the reception is a generous double bedroom with built-in wardrobes and a large sash window overlooking the lightwell.A corridor with excellent built-in storage leads to the rear of the flat and into the lovely open-plan kitchen and reception room. The kitchen is fitted with sleek wall and base units and fully integrated appliances. The space offers ample room for a dining table and a comfortable seating area, currently arranged as a study. The room is filled with natural light from rooflights, a glass door to the lightwell, and Crittall-style windows and double doors that open onto the patio garden. This beautifully presented three-bedroom home offers nearly 1,000 sq. ft. of stylish living space finished with Farrow & Ball décor, oak flooring and a wonderful flow of light. Highlights include a rustic kitchen with range cooker, a converted loft with luxury bathroom, and a south-facing garden bursting with fruit trees and roses. Perfectly located for young families and first-time buyers, you’re a short walk from Langthorne Park, the Olympic Park and East Village’s cafés, bakeries and restaurants, while Stratford International is on your doorstep with high-speed trains whisking you to King’s Cross in just seven minutes.IF YOU LIVED HERE…A Light-Filled Family Home with Style and Character. Decorated with Farrow & Ball throughout, this charming home offers nearly 1,000 sq. ft. of immaculate living space, finished with tasteful décor, thoughtful fittings, and a wonderful flow of natural light.On the ground floor, generous bay windows, oak flooring, and a carefully chosen colour palette create a warm and inviting atmosphere. The kitchen sits proudly at the front, blending rustic character with modern comfort. From the range cooker and hand-painted tiles to bespoke shutters, column radiators, and charming wall lights, every detail has been considered. A ground floor shower room and utility add a touch of everyday convenience for busy family life.Step outside into your south-facing garden, a true sanctuary. Bursting with mature planting, it boasts a magnificent black cherry tree laden with fruit in May, followed by plums, greengages, apples, and pears across the seasons. At the entrance, a spectacular Gertrude Jekyll rose blooms twice yearly, filling the garden with scent and offering an abundance of cut flowers for the home.Upstairs, the first floor features a beautifully finished master bedroom alongside a versatile single room, perfect for a nursery or home office. The converted loft provides a spacious double bedroom and a stunning bathroom, complete with Fired Earth encaustic floor tiles, a Neptune sink, and a striking cast-iron bath.The location offers the best of both worlds. For green space, you’re just five minutes from Langthorne Park with its mature trees and pond, or you are a 15-minute stroll from the Olympic Park with cafés, restaurants, and sports facilities from the 2012 Games legacy, including Lee Valley VeloPark and the London Aquatics Centre.As for food and drink, you’re spoilt for choice. features: OPEN HOUSE 1 Nov from 10-12pm, book to reserve your space
A bold, pink, park-facing freehold, full of creativity
Directly opposite Wormholt Park with a Front & Back Garden

description: Do you like parks? Urban design? Your very own freehold house? Do you like pink? Do you like personality? If so, this might just be your perfect home.Opposite Wormholt Park, this bold and creative West London mid-terrace celebrates a pared-back, industrial-chic aesthetic with intentional restraint, an exposed iron beam, open-plan living, and custom pressed OSB panelling forming part of its raw yet refined contemporary finish.From the main bedroom, the view is something special: a wide, lush sweep of green that changes with the seasons. It’s the first thing you see when you wake up in the morning, a reminder that you’re surrounded by nature even in the middle of London. Park views like this are a rare luxury. In most parts of the city you’re staring into someone else’s window. Here, you wake up to open sky and shifting colour.And best of all, it’s a freehold house without compromise. Not a cramped flat with neighbours above and below, but your own space, front to back. You have a lovely private garden with no one overlooking, big open sky, and a real sense of light and openness. Inside, there’s a generous master bedroom with that double-sized feel, open-plan living downstairs, and at the end of the garden, an architect-designed garden room that’s fully powered with underfloor heating and air conditioning. It already functions beautifully as a home office, creative studio, gym, entertainment and storage space.The ground floor is open and sociable. The hallway walls have been removed so you step straight into the lounge, flowing seamlessly into the kitchen at the back. Polished concrete floors meet bespoke joinery and design-led detailing, highlighted by bright yellow accents and a cleverly concealed utility cupboard for the washer and dryer. The kitchen includes a filtered water tap, combining utility with thoughtful design. The house is fully connected, with lighting, heating, and entertainment systems that can be controlled by voice or app. Every switch and bulb was chosen for seamless integration and modern living. The blinds throughout the house are automated, adding another layer of comfort and convenience.A pink staircase runner joyfully leads to two bedrooms and a family bathroom with a shower over the bath.Planning permission is already approved for an architect-designed rear extension that would expand the home to around 1,200 square feet, giving new owners room to grow while maintaining its open, modern feel.Families are spoiled for choice when it comes to outstanding local schools, from creative, community-minded primaries to some of the highest-rated academies in the UK, all within easy walking distance. Many nearby schools have a strong focus on creativity, wellbeing, and exceptional food education.Everyday life here feels easy and connected. Westfield, White City, and Soho House are close by for shopping, dining, and culture, and at the end of the street there’s a beautifully designed GP surgery built into the park itself with excellent doctors, nurses, and therapists providing a real community feel. There’s also a Sainsbury’s Local just at the top of the road, and you’re within strolling distance of Askew Road, lined with independent shops, cafés, and neighbourhood favourites.Transport links are exceptional. description: This beautifully presented Victorian terrace offers three bedrooms, two bathrooms and a refined balance of period character and modern design. Light-filled interiors flow across elegant living spaces finished with engineered wooden flooring and thoughtful detailing. The modern kitchen and bathrooms bring a fresh, polished edge, while the layout provides flexibility for family life. Perfectly positioned within walking distance of Leytonstone Station and surrounded by an excellent selection of cafés and pubs, this home combines convenience with style. There’s also exciting potential for further development, subject to planning, making it an appealing opportunity in one of East London’s most desirable neighbourhoods.IF YOU LIVED HERE…The soft grey exterior, framed with white detailing, gives the home a quiet grace. A tiled path leads to a beautiful stained-glass front door, where light and colour spill into the hallway, setting the tone for the tranquil, well-balanced interiors beyond.At the front, the bay-windowed reception is filled with daylight, its plantation shutters and pale walls lending a soothing sense of space, ideal for relaxed evenings or gatherings with friends. Further inside, the second reception exudes warmth and character, perfectly suited for dining or unwinding, with a built-in cupboard adding subtle practicality. The adjoining kitchen is light and modern, its neutral palette and easy flow leading towards the garden. There is scope here to open into the side return and connect the rear spaces to form a striking open-plan kitchen and dining area overlooking the garden, subject to planning permission.The ground floor bathroom continues the home’s serene aesthetic, with neutral tiling, dark accents, and a full-length bath with overhead shower. Beyond, the south-east facing garden offers a peaceful blend of greenery and privacy, featuring a paved terrace, neat lawn, and a shaded seating area framed by mature trees.Upstairs, three wonderfully balanced bedrooms provide generous comfort and style. The main bedroom is soft and airy with shuttered windows, the second bright and spacious, and the third calm and restful, each offering a distinct sense of harmony. A fresh, two-tone family bathroom completes the first floor with understated refinement.Set in a vibrant pocket of East London, this location offers the perfect balance of community charm and city convenience. Just around the corner, Francis Road reflects the area’s strong local spirit, with its independent boutiques, weekend market stalls, and much-loved spots such as Yardarm, Marmelo and The Northcote Arms. A little further, Leytonstone High Road provides a great mix of everyday amenities along with the ever-popular Wild Goose Bakery for relaxed brunches or coffee with friends. Green spaces are close at hand too, with Wanstead Flats, part of the historic Epping Forest, offering a vast landscape for weekend walks, and the Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park just beyond. Families are well served by excellent schools, including the Outstanding-rated Newport Primary School only a four-minute walk away.WHAT ELSE?Transport links are excellent, with Leytonstone Station just a twelve-minute walk away, offering Central line services direct to Liverpool Street, the West End and beyond. Leytonstone High Road Station is around a ten-minute walk, providing easy Overground connections. Numerous bus routes link the area to nearby neighbourhoods including Wanstead, Leyton, Stratford and Walthamstow.Reception - 3.55 x 3.6 (11'7" x 11'9") - Reception - 4.47 x 3.03 (14'7" x 9'11") - Storage - Kitchen - 2.7 x 3 (8'10" x 9'10") - Bathroom - Bedroom - 4.57 x 3 (14'11" x 9'10") - Bedroom - 3.55 x 2.12 (11'7" x 6'11") - Bedroom - 2.73 x 3 (8'11" x 9'10") - Bathroom - Garden - 4.5 x 9 (14'9" x 29'6") - A WORD FROM THE OWNER...This has been a much-loved home for the past five years, and we will be sad to leave. A 30-foot through living space forms the heart of the home, light-filled and generous, with original fireplaces and a calm, natural palette that creates a sense of openness and ease. It's a space equally suited to quiet family days or lively gatherings. The kitchen is fitted with bespoke forest green cabinetry and a curated range of premium Smeg and Samsung appliances. Finished with an eye for detail and longevity, it opens onto a private courtyard garden, a tranquil outdoor space that includes a traditional pizza oven, perfect for relaxed evenings or weekend lunches with friends. Upstairs, there are three bedrooms, each offering a peaceful retreat, with thoughtful finishes that reflect the home's quiet elegance and enduring charm. The bathroom continues the home's quiet elegance, with glazed metro tiling, a walk-in shower, and a heated towel rail, all selected to complement the building's character while offering modern comfort. A large cellar offers excellent storage, helping to maintain clean, open living spaces above. Whilst renovating, the owners discovered a non-operational well beneath the property, which was assessed and covered up. Buyers are encouraged to make their own enquiries. Beyond the home itself, Circus Street is known for its friendly and welcoming atmosphere. Neighbours greet each other by name, and there's a strong sense of community, with a mix of families, young professionals and couples, also long-term residents all sharing in the life of this cherished street. This is a rare opportunity to own a home on one of Greenwich's most desirable roads, a place where thoughtful design, community, and history come together with quiet confidence. West Greenwich is one of London's most treasured and historic neighbourhoods, a place where rich heritage, architectural beauty, and a strong sense of community quietly shape everyday life. Set just moments from Greenwich Park and the town centre, this part of Greenwich has long been sought after for its handsome period streets, leafy surroundings, and proximity to the River Thames. The area forms part of a designated conservation zone, preserving its Georgian and Victorian character while fostering a village-like feel that's increasingly rare in the capital. Life here moves at a gentler pace, but everything you need is within easy reach. The independent shops, cafés, and markets of Greenwich town offer an eclectic, creative energy with favourites like the historic Greenwich Market, the Picturehouse cinema, and a wide range of restaurants and bakeries all just a short walk away. The riverside and Royal Park provide an abundance of green space, open views, and moments of stillness in nature. West Greenwich is exceptionally well connected. The nearby Greenwich DLR station offers quick and direct access to Canary Wharf and the City, making it an ideal location for commuters who want to balance city life with peaceful surroundings. Just a few minutes' walk from the house, Greenwich Station offers quick and convenient connections, reaching London Bridge in under 10 minutes for easy access to Borough Market, the Southbank, and Tate Modern, or London Cannon Street in around 16 minutes. There are also direct services to Kent and the coastal town of Ramsgate, making travel in and out of the city simple and flexible. Additionally, close neighbours Blackheath and Deptford bring their own unique character, with Blackheath's vast open commons and quaint village atmosphere, and Deptford's thriving arts scene and markets enriching the local vibe. The proximity to the River Thames adds another layer of appeal, with scenic walks along the riverbank and a range of waterside cafés and pubs offering a relaxed retreat from the bustle of the city. A truly exceptional home, and one that will undoubtedly capture the imagination of anyone looking for something unique. This stunning apartment forms part of a Grade II listed church conversion within the Royal Military Academy, one of SE18’s most iconic and historic gated developments. Once known as the Church of St Michael and All Angels, this newly created masterpiece is one of only three brand-new apartments within the former church, a rare and extraordinary opportunity to own a piece of architectural history reimagined for modern living.The foundation stone was laid in 1902, and the building’s magnificent Gothic Revival architecture has been sensitively restored to showcase the splendour of its original craftsmanship, now fused with the sophistication of contemporary design.A grand set of double timber doors open into a majestic entrance, a portico adorned with stained glass windows, panelled walls, and ornate detailing that immediately set the tone. The main living space is awe-inspiring, soaring vaulted timber ceilings hung with chandelier lights, stone mullion stained glass windows, and a sense of scale that’s rarely seen, spanning some 1,837 sq ft in total. The flooring is ceramic tiled with underfloor heating, and the timber wall panelling tells a story of the regiments that once worshipped here.The kitchen, finished in elegant navy blue cabinetry with an array of modern appliances, strikes the perfect balance between heritage and high-end modernity. Also on the ground floor are a guest WC and a separate utility room, both designed with thoughtful practicality.Upstairs, the mezzanine bedrooms overlook the grand living area below, offering mesmerising views of the church’s architecture and the stained glass windows above. The principal bedroom includes a luxurious en-suite shower room, while the separate family bathroom features a freestanding bath, perfect for indulgent relaxation.Positioned on the edge of Woolwich Common, the Royal Military Academy remains one of South London’s most recognisable historic landmarks, steeped in history and grandeur, yet beautifully adapted for contemporary life. The development offers 24-hour concierge, wide tree-lined boulevards, manicured lawns, and even a cricket pitch, reputed to be one of the oldest in the country.Perfectly placed for connectivity, the home enjoys access to excellent transport links, including the Elizabeth Line at Woolwich for fast journeys to Canary Wharf, The City, West End, and Heathrow. Woolwich Arsenal and Dockyard stations also provide quick rail and DLR services across London. Nearby, Blackheath Village, Charlton Village, and The Royal Standard offer an appealing mix of independent shops, cafes, and restaurants, complementing Woolwich’s vibrant town centre.This is a home that goes far beyond expectation, historic, dramatic, and truly one of a kind.DisclaimerFelicity J Lord Estate Agents also offer a professional, ARLA accredited Lettings and Management Service. location: Chapel Farm Road, Mottingham, London, SE9
description: With four bedrooms, a bathroom, shower, utility room, large open plan kitchen/living/dining room and two further reception rooms this is a spacious (1,723 sq.ft.) family home. The location is also good with local shops and Mottingham Station both just half a mile. Dorset Road Infants and Ravensworth Primary schools are within walking distance, a 11 and 9 minute walk respectively. The property benefits from a driveway with enough space to provide off street parking for several cars and has an EV charger. Once inside, the hallway has a useful understairs storage cupboard and WC and gives access to all the ground floor accommodation. At the front of the property are two reception rooms. The living room features a bay window and built-in bookcases with a handy desk area. The second room offers great flexibility and could be used as a TV snug, playroom, or study depending on your lifestyle.To the rear and spanning the whole width of the property is the heart of the house, an open plan kitchen/living/dining room which is divided into distinct zones. The kitchen is fitted with deep blue wall and base cupboards and a matching island unit, the worktops are quartz. Some integrated appliances are to remain and there is a butlers sink. The space easily accommodates a dining table and chairs as well as comfy lounge furniture. Bi-fold doors, a window to the rear and three roof lanterns bring in plenty of natural light and make this room bright and welcoming and ideal for family living and entertaining.A utility room with storage cupboards and shower complete the ground floor accommodation.Upstairs on the first floor are four bedrooms all with wood laminate flooring. Bedrooms one and two are doubles and both have fitted wardrobes. Bedroom 3 to the front is a small double and is currently being used as a study. Bedroom 4, a single overlooks the rear garden.The family bathroom has the airing cupboard and is fitted with a white suite and comprises a WC, wash hand basin with cupboard storage beneath and a bath with shower. There is grey panelling to the side of the bath and large format tiles to the walls and floors. Windows provide light and ventilation.Outside to the rear, decking leads directly from the house with steps down to the lawn. At the far end of the garden, an area of decking and paving, framed by raised beds, creates a perfect spot for BBQs and relaxing outdoors. description: The Signal Townhouse development is collection of stylish modern townhouses situated in the heart of East Greenwich. features: Three Double Bedroom Arts and Crafts style Home (1132 SQFT)
description: Rarely available is this stunning Arts and Crafts style 1132 SQFT Three Double Family Home boasting a Special and Large Mature Garden. These properties rarely come to the market, there are only 6 of them in the terrace and you have to go back almost 20 years before the last one was sold! Upon arrival you are greeted with a black gate and red door with Georgian style glass in the top half. There is stunning original style terracotta and cream tiles along with an immaculate front garden with its established roses and shrubs that give complete privacy. The ground floor features a bright and welcoming hallway with cornicing and dado rails, a wider-than-average living/dining room with lovely bay window, feature fireplace and access to the garden along with a fitted kitchen with dining area with doors leading directly out to a stunning raised terrace with a glass roof. Thereby enabling the full width of the breakfast room via its French doors to be open, leading out immediately to the garden with its covered space. There is downstairs WC perfectly located off the hallway. The first floor provides three good size double bedrooms with two of them featuring bespoke built in wardrobes. The main bedroom has two sets of built in cupboards which stretch from the floor to the ceiling, there is also a lovely original fireplace along with a glorious arched window with verdant views over the front garden.The loft area is huge and provides exciting potential to create further space by adding a 4th bedroom with an en suite. There is also potential for a ground floor extension. What makes this property so special is the garden. It’s over 80 ft long and has been landscaped to provide several zones, each perfect for soaking up the beauty and tranquillity. There is a built-in underground water irrigation system along with several electrical power points. There are numerous lights which are solar powered. The current owner has even installed a Wi-Fi extender ensuring that even at the back of your 80ft garden, you have internet connection! There are several LED lights in the shed and also by the seating area at the bottom of the garden. description: Granville Gardens Plot 5 is a 1432 sq.ft, four bedroom townhouse with large double bedrooms, a patio and a terrace, plus allocated parking. Opportunities like Granville Gardens don't come along very often, all homes feature a stunning specification and sensational contemporary architecture set within beautifully landscaped gardens, for everyone to enjoy. The appeal to every home is enhanced by being designed to maximise the space and light. Featuring a stunning specification including fully fitted Brandt gloss kitchens, stylish amtico flooring and porcelanosa floor and wall tiles to your bathrooms and en-suites. As much thought has gone into the space outside of every home as has gone into inside. There are beautiful communal gardens to enjoy and socialise with neighbours and friends, however, every home also features its own private space, you can relax on your balcony, terrace or garden. The haven of peace you've been looking for that's only 25 mins from Central London. A hidden gem tucked away from the hustle and bustle. Location - Step out of your new home, and you can be in the thriving and richly diverse neighborhoods of Golders Green and Hampstead Heath before you know it. - Locally you are spoilt for choice for places to eat with fantastic restaurants including the family friendly Italian Pizaza or Soyo for a taste of the Mediterranean. - Head to nearby Brent Cross to satisfy all your retail therapy cravings, featuring designer and high street stores. - For a walk and to take in the fresh air, grab a coffee from Rea's Place in Childs Hill Park, just moments from Granville Gardens. Or head a little further afield for the stunning Hill Gardens and Pergola in Golders Hill Park, or why not take a dip in the bathing ponds in Hampstead Heath! Transport - Back into the city or out further afield: wherever you need to get to, your choice of public transport will get you there. - Golders Green tube station (Zone 3) is just a short walk or bus journey away, with excellent links to the city via the Northern Line. You can reach Euston in just 22 minutes, Kings Cross in 23 minutes or London Waterloo in just 30 minutes. - Cricklewood mainline station is also close by and provides frequent services - with trains between Wimbledon and St Albans, Moorgate and Luton. - If you would prefer to take the bus, you are spoilt for choice with roughly 20 buses every hour taking you into the city and beyond. description: Keating Estates are proud to present to market a remarkable four double-bedroom, two-bathroom period house, located on a quiet and peaceful street between the amenities of Clapham and Brixton.Full Description - Keating Estates are proud to present to market a remarkable four double-bedroom, two-bathroom period house, located on a quiet and peaceful street between the amenities of Clapham and Brixton. This charming home sits within a popular residential pocket of pretty Victorian terraces, equidistant from both sought-after locations, and will appeal to purchasers considering a move to either area. Lovingly modernised and extended by its current owners in recent years, the property has been enhanced with great sensitivity to its period character. From the moment you arrive, the handsome Victorian façade sets a welcoming tone. The classic brickwork and detailing create instant kerb appeal, while the pretty pink front door provides a cheerful and distinctive touch that sets the scene for the warmth and creativity found throughout this home.Upon entering, the attractive tiled entrance hall leads into a stunning open-plan double reception and dining room that flows beautifully from front to back. This impressive through-space showcases a perfect balance between contemporary living and period elegance, featuring two decorative fireplaces, elegant ceiling roses, and bespoke cabinetry built neatly into the alcoves. A wide bay window fitted with traditional sash windows bathes the room in natural light, creating an inviting atmosphere ideal for both entertaining and relaxation. The thoughtful layout and neutral tones enhance the sense of space and flow, while subtle design details retain the building’s Victorian soul.Beyond the reception, the heart of the home lies in the beautifully extended kitchen, a striking yet harmonious blend of modern design and classic charm. Painted in a soft pink, the walls provide a warm backdrop to the bold blue geometric floor tiles, which add depth and character. Sleek wooden cabinetry offers ample storage, complemented by a contemporary blue-tiled splashback and integrated appliances, including a Miele oven and gas hob. A traditional butler sink enjoys lovely views over the rear garden, and overhead clerestory windows flood the space with sunlight while maintaining privacy. There’s also room for a breakfast table, creating the perfect spot for informal dining. Glazed French doors open directly onto the garden, ensuring a seamless transition between indoor and outdoor living.South-facing, the garden is a delightful suntrap and the ideal setting for morning coffees, evening drinks, or summer barbecues. Thoughtfully landscaped with planted borders and easy-to-maintain paving, it offers a low-maintenance oasis that feels a world away from the hustle and bustle of city life. This outdoor space perfectly complements the calm and inviting atmosphere of the interior.Ascending to the first floor, the principal bedroom spans the full width of the property, offering an elegant and tranquil retreat. Painted in a bold and sophisticated blue, this luxurious bedroom combines period charm with modern comfort. A decorative fireplace acts as a focal point, while bespoke wardrobes have been fitted neatly into the alcoves to maximise storage without compromising style. Multiple sash windows flood the space with natural light, highlighting the soft carpeting underfoot and creating a serene, airy feel.A second double bedroom overlooks the peaceful garden to the rear of the property. This charming room benefits from its own en-suite W/C and basin, providing convenience and privacy for guests or family members. Adjacent to this room is the well-appointed family bathroom, neutrally decorated and designed with both practicality and relaxation in mind.A cleverly designed loft extension completes the accommodation, transforming the upper level into a bright and versatile space. Here, two further double bedrooms have been created, the front bedroom featuring multiple skylights, and the rear benefiting from striking city views. These rooms could easily serve as children’s bedrooms, guest rooms, or even a home office or studio, offering flexibility to suit a variety of lifestyles. The second bathroom on this floor is beautifully finished, featuring a sleek walk-in shower and high-quality fittings. Eaves storage provides an excellent solution for housing lesser-used items while keeping the main spaces tidy and uncluttered.With over 1,450 square feet of internal living space, this family home is beautifully light and airy, boasting a stylish finish throughout. It’s ideal for buyers seeking a turnkey property that combines the charm of a period home with the comfort and efficiency of modern design.The location is equally impressive. This peaceful and highly sought-after road is prized for its community feel and proximity to both Brixton and Clapham. The lively bars, restaurants, and cafés of Acre Lane are just a short walk away, while within an eleven-minute stroll lies everything Brixton has to offer, from the iconic music venues and ever-popular Brixton Village to large retailers and excellent transport links. In the other direction, Clapham High Street provides a vibrant mix of nightlife, shops, and eateries, as well as quick access to the Northern Line via Clapham North, Clapham Common, and Clapham South stations. The Victoria Line at Brixton offers fast connections across the city.For those who enjoy green open spaces, both Brockwell Park and Clapham Common are within easy reach, offering scenic walking routes, tennis courts, and cafés. The charming Abbeville Village, with its independent boutiques and eateries, is also nearby.Freehold.Chain- free.BrochuresMargate Road, SW2Brochure

features: Easy Access to Highams Park and Walthamstow
description: You’ll find this three-bedroom modern mid-terrace home unfolding over 1,144 sq ft - plenty of room to spread out and make it your own, with the bonus of potential to extend even further (subject to the usual planning permissions). Inside, you’ve got three reception spaces to play with, plus a generous 45-foot, south-facing garden that’s a real sun trap. Out front, your private driveway keeps life simple, avoiding parking dramas - just grab your keys and go. Commuting’s a breeze too, with Highams Park Station only a 7-minute walk away and direct trains to Liverpool Street in under 25 minutes. Local shops, cafés, and daily essentials are all close by, and when you’re craving a bit of greenery, Epping Forest is just a short stroll away.IF YOU LIVED HERE.......Highams Park has a rare balance - quick City connections paired with green space that’s genuinely on your doorstep. It’s easy to see why it’s been named one of The Times’ best places to live. Pull straight onto your own private driveway, no circling the block or parking headaches, just an easy hop from car to front door, kids, shopping and all. Step inside, and the kitchen greets you first, which feels especially welcoming when you’re ready for that first cuppa. Tiled in royal blue with country-style wooden cabinetry, it’s a proper, practical space that does exactly what you need it to.From here, the house opens into three reception rooms, the largest stretching almost 22 feet and flooded with light from generous dual-aspect windows. Engineered wood flooring runs throughout, tying the whole space together with an easy, cohesive flow. The two additional receptions can be whatever you want them to be; keep them as a dining room and snug like the current owners, or switch things up to suit your own rhythm of life.A glazed door leads out to the garden, where a paved patio sets the scene for outdoor meals. Beyond that, a neat lawn stretches towards a wooded boundary framed by mature trees, with the open green space of Wadham Sports Ground just beyond creating a space that’s peaceful, private, and full of greenery.Upstairs, there are three bedrooms - two comfortable doubles (one with built-in louvred wardrobes ready to go) and a bright single, all with natural wood floors and leafy views over the trees behind the house. A separate family bathroom and WC take the stress out of busy mornings, with white marble-effect tiles, a bath and shower combo, and a modern vanity sink keeping everything clean-lined and functional.WHAT ELSE?Epping Forest, London's biggest green space covering 5900 acres is on your doorstep, providing plenty of opportunities for walks, cycling, and picnics in nature.For those interested in history, the Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge is within easy reach. This well-preserved Tudor building offers a glimpse into England's past and provides panoramic views over Epping Forest.Pop over to YAZ or Breeze for delectable Mediterranean delights. Or enjoy evening drinks at trendy micro-pub, The Stag and Lantern or try out the family-run bar, Vino Tap.By car, the North Circular Road is around a mile/5 minutes away, with the M25 just under 7 miles/20 minutes.Reception Room 1 - 3.85 x 6.61 (12'7" x 21'8") - Reception Room 2 - 4.04 x 3.08 (13'3" x 10'1") - Reception Room 3 - 3.65 x 2.30 (11'11" x 7'6") - Kitchen - 3.13 x 2.82 (10'3" x 9'3") - Bathroom - 2.21 x 1.49 (7'3" x 4'10") - Bedroom 1 - 3.05 x 4.04 (10'0" x 13'3") - Bedroom 2 - 3.00 x 3.11 (9'10" x 10'2") - Bedroom 3 - 1.90 x 3.11 (6'2" x 10'2") - Garden - 8.1 x 13.6 (26'6" x 44'7") - A WORD FROM THE EXPERT..... "I love the outdoors as much as the buzz of the city, so Chingford is perfect for me. With Epping Forest on the doorstep, cycling, hiking and exploring are only a hop away, while great transport links take you into the city in under half an hour.Switching between Chingford Mount and North Chingford for a pub, café or takeaway means I’m spoilt for choice. I often meet friends and family at The Rusty Bike for Thai food, a wide beer selection or a game of football.Weekends are for the Kings Head, with its sunny beer garden and cosy roasts in winter. Travelling between appointments is a joy thanks to the friendly community, you’re often greeted with a smile. The area’s diversity makes it special, with international takeaways and supermarkets offering new tastes to discover.Evenings are for dog walks at Yates Meadow, where peaceful hills meet city skylines. Chingford really does offer something for everyone, and it’s more affordable than most of London." description: Measuring 1,761 sq. ft. this stunning five-bedroom, Victorian semi detached family home combines stylish modern interiors and period detailing, located on the Harringay Ladder. As you arrive, you’re greeted by an attractive painted facade, a neat front garden, and a welcoming blue front door. Inside, the ground floor features an elegant double reception room, with stripped-wood floors, ornate ceiling mouldings, bespoke cabinetry, and a cosy wood-burning stove. To the rear, a bright and practical family kitchen and morning room, with ample space for dining and entertaining. French doors open onto a beautifully maintained south-facing garden. The first floor offers three bedrooms, including a spacious bay-fronted room, alongside a sleek and modern family bathroom and built in storage in the hallway. The top floor is an impressive loft conversion, finished with remarkable attention to detail. Here you’ll find a luxurious master suite with en suite bathroom, plus a generous double bedroom complete with Juliet balcony. Set on the sought-after Harringay Ladder, the location offers a vibrant community spirit, handsome Victorian terraces, and excellent amenities. Green Lanes is moments away, home to an ever-growing mix of independent shops, cafés and restaurants, including favourites such as Café Snug, The Dusty Knuckle, Brouhaha and the iconic Salisbury Pub. The much-loved Harringay Local Store is also nearby, while nearby Crouch End and Stroud Green provide even more choice. Transport links are superb, with Turnpike Lane station (Piccadilly Line) just a minute’s walk, and nearby Harringay and Hornsey stations offering direct services to Finsbury Park, Kings Cross, Old Street and Moorgate. Families will also appreciate the property’s proximity to highly regarded nurseries and North Harringay School.Please contact the Sales department at Winkworth Harringay office to arrange an appointment to view - Winkworth.co.uk Your local independently owned property agency with a network of London offices. A remarkable loft-style apartment set within the historic Lamb Brewery Studios — one of West London’s most distinctive landmarks on the banks of the River Thames. Originally built in 1901 as part of the former Fullers Brewery, the building has been meticulously converted to create a collection of exceptional residences blending industrial heritage with contemporary design. Extending to approximately 1,277 sq ft, The Bottle House offers superb lateral space and an abundance of natural light. The expansive open-plan kitchen, living and dining area showcases soaring ceilings, exposed brickwork and a wealth of character, creating an impressive setting for both everyday living and entertaining. There are three generous double bedrooms and two stylish bathrooms, including a striking principal suite with a vaulted ceiling that once supported the original brewery water tanks — a stunning architectural feature and a nod to the building’s fascinating history. Unique Combination Opportunity: We are also marketing The Water Tower, a magnificent adjacent apartment measuring 1,390 sq ft. Together, these two properties present a truly rare chance to combine and create a breathtaking 2,667 sq ft ‘super flat’ with exceptional volume and spectacular river views.SituationDiscreetly positioned within a private cobbled mews off Church Street, the Lamb Brewery Studios are moments from the Thames Path, historic riverside pubs and independent cafés. Chiswick High Road and Hammersmith are within walking distance, offering an excellent choice of shops, restaurants and transport links, including Turnham Green Underground Station (District & Piccadilly Lines). The M3 and M4 motorways, Heathrow Airport and central London are all easily accessible. The area is also well served by leading schools including St Paul’s, Godolphin, Latymer, Colet Court, Harrodian and St Paul’s Girls’. Property Ref Number: HAM-61620

Do You Dream Of A Handmade Kitchen That Opens Onto A Jasmine Scented Garden, With The Buzz Of Peckham Just Steps Away? A superb five bedroom Victorian mid-terrace, with bespoke touches and a 20m south-facing garden - and so close to SE15’s best spots.It’s true - you can have a dream kitchen, a gorgeous 20m garden and a quiet but convenient street, right in the heart of Peckham.The kitchen’s been extended with care and superb attention to detail - creating the social hub of this stunning family home. Full-length skylights and custom green-framed doors flood the space with light. Handmade cabinets, restored tiles, a larder, wine fridge and quartz worktops keep things both functional and beautiful. There’s underfloor heating too, though the south-facing aspect means the sun often does that job for you.That flow into the garden is what makes it so special. It’s almost 20 metres long, low-maintenance and full of interest year-round - jasmine and magnolia, tree cover for privacy, and a real sense of calm. Inside, the open plan lounge and dining room offers more space to spread out, with high ceilings, original pine floors and built-in storage. A warm, terracotta tone from Edward Bulmer brings it all together. There’s a separate w/c down here as well, with bold Josef Frank wallpaper and a tucked-away cupboard.The cellar adds a generous chunk of extra storage - or future potential.Upstairs, the first floor has three double bedrooms, a family bathroom with handmade Mexican tiles and a laundry cupboard by the bedrooms (such a thoughtful touch). The guest room here features India Yellow paint and a fireplace infilled with unique hand-painted tiles - a small detail, but a lovely one nonetheless.The second floor offers two more double bedrooms, including a light-filled top-floor suite with city views, and its own four piece bathroom with skylight and pocket door. The other room is currently used as a dressing room, but could easily flex back to a bedroom or office.Throughout the house, the feeling is consistent: warm, considered, and full of character. Every space has been finished with care, but nothing feels overdone or too “designed”.Bearing in mind the central Peckham address, the location is a quiet surprise. You’re only a four minute walk from Peckham Rye station, with quick links to London Bridge, Victoria and the Overground. Rye Lane’s bars, Bellenden Road’s indie boutiques and Queen’s Road’s restaurants are all within a 10 minute walk - yet the street feels calm and quiet.And if you know the area, you’ll know that “Peckham House” the 2024 RIBA London House of the Year nominee is just at the end of the road - another quiet reminder that this is a pocket of real quality.If you love getting outdoors, Peckham Rye Park and Common (with over 100 acres of green space) is also just up the road. Perfect for working up an appetite.This pretty Victorian mid-terrace is a rare one: combining space and style, right in the middle of everything - but somehow quietly removed from it all too.We’re expecting plenty of interest in this stunning family home so get in touch to arrange a private viewing.A superb five bedroom Victorian mid-terrace with handmade kitchen and 20m garden in central PeckhamExtended kitchen-diner with skylights, larder, wine fridge and bespoke cabinetry throughoutSouth-facing garden nearly 20m long, with jasmine, magnolia and mature trees for privacyOpen plan lounge and dining space with original floors, built-in storage and high ceilingsFive double bedrooms including a light-filled top floor suite with four piece ensuite and city viewsGorgeous family bathroom with handmade Mexican tiles plus upstairs laundry cupboard Downstairs w/c with Josef Frank wallpaper, tucked-away storage and underfloor heatingOnly a four minute walk to Peckham Rye station with quick links to London Bridge, Victoria and OvergroundWalk to Bellenden Road, Rye Lane or Queen’s Road for restaurants, cafés and local favouritesA short walk from Peckham Rye Common - over 100 acres of green space to exploreEPC Rating: C

description: A semi-detached five bedroom family home. DescriptionNestled just off Streatham Common, this exceptional five-bedroom semi-detached family home combines period charm with contemporary design, offering beautifully presented interiors, generous proportions, and an enviable south-west facing garden — perfect for modern family living.Upon entering through the stained-glass front door, you’re welcomed by a long, elegant hallway with striking tessellated tile flooring and a sweeping staircase. To the left, a stunning reception room showcases a large bay window with plantation shutters, intricate cornicing, an original fireplace, and bespoke alcove shelving. A teal feature wall adds a touch of sophistication, while the wooden flooring and high ceilings enhance the sense of light and space.To the rear, the heart of the home unfolds — a spectacular kitchen and family living space with ceilings over three metres high. The kitchen is finished in a chic monochrome palette, featuring black cabinetry, marble worktops, and a matching island that serves as a stylish centrepiece. The adjoining dining and sitting area boasts an original fireplace with wood-burning stove, a ceiling rose, and French doors opening onto the beautiful private garden.The south-west facing garden, extending approximately 40ft, offers paved seating areas ideal for outdoor dining, a lawned section for play, and a charming conservatory-style outbuilding with additional storage and side access. A cosy snug and convenient WC complete the ground floor.Upstairs, the first floor comprises three immaculate double bedrooms and a luxurious four-piece family bathroom with roll-top bath and neutral tiling. The principal bedroom features air conditioning, wooden flooring, and a large bay window with stained-glass detail, along with access to a stylish shower room. The second floor provides two further double bedrooms, a modern bathroom, and excellent loft storage with potential for extension (STPP).Additional features include off-street parking, air conditioning to key bedrooms, and retained period details throughout — from cornicing to fireplaces and original wood floors.Perfectly positioned for families, this home is just moments from Streatham Common, excellent local schools, and transport links, offering a seamless blend of space, elegance, and convenience.LocationSituated just off Streatham Common, this superb family home enjoys a quietly residential enclave while remaining well connected.Streatham Common railway station is 0.5 miles away, in Zone 3, offering regular Southern services to Clapham Junction, Victoria, London Bridge and other central destinations. Streatham station (Thameslink) lies at approximately 0.3 miles’ distance, providing direct east–west links across London. The property benefits from proximity to Streatham Common green space and recreation, while local shopping, cafés, and amenities are all within comfortable walking distance.Road access is convenient via the A23 corridor, connecting to central and south London arterial routes. Streatham High Road (approx. 0.3 miles) offers supermarkets including Sainsbury’s and Marks & Spencer Foodhall, alongside a wide range of cafés, restaurants, and independent shops.Streatham Common and The Rookery Gardens are close, providing extensive parkland, playgrounds, and walking routes.Square Footage: 2,450 sq ft BrochuresWeb Details
