Set on the highly sought-after Leconfield Road in the heart of Newington Green, this beautifully proportioned four-bedroom period home offers an exciting opportunity to own a substantial, character-rich residence that is currently undergoing a thoughtful renovation. Set across three levels and measuring approximately 1,322 sq ft (123 m²), the home blends timeless architectural features with the potential for stylish, modern finishes. Just a short stroll from Highbury & Islington Station with easy access to the Victoria Line, Overground, and National Rail—perfect for commuters and explorers.THE PROPERTYUpon entering the home at ground level, you’re welcomed by a classic Victorian hallway that leads directly into the main living areas. The front bedroom room, bathed in natural light from the bay window, features elegant proportions, ideal for both entertaining and relaxing. Just beyond, a second generously sized double bedroom sits quietly to the rear, overlooking the garden, and conveniently adjacent to a large, stylishly appointed family bathroom, positioned at the rear corner of the property. The properties bathroom has a delightful finish. Bathrooms are rarely defined as spectacles but that word suites here! With floor to ceiling tilingm a freestanding bathtub along with a walk in shower enclosure. The ceramic double sink comes with its own vanity storage unit. Descending to the lower ground floor, the heart of the home unfolds into an expansive open-plan layout. A dedicated dining area connects seamlessly with the spacious kitchen at the rear, which opens directly onto the private rear garden—a peaceful outdoor haven stretching to a good size for the patch. At the front of this level is a substantial reception area, ideal for family gatherings, a home cinema, or a spacious formal lounge. This level is rich in potential for everyday living and entertaining, with clear zoning for kitchen, dining, and relaxation. Set up with bespoke light fittings to create different moods in the spaces. Ascending to the First Floor an additional two bedrooms, all well-proportioned and filled with natural light. At the front of the house lies the second bedroom with dual sash windows that create a bright and airy atmosphere. Throughout the home, period details such as tall ceilings, bay windows, and generous room dimensions create a feeling of refined grandeur.The property is moments from Newingtons vibrant community, local parks, cafés, transport links, and top-rated schools. Whether you’re looking for a forever family home or a stylish London base, Leconfield Road promises space, potential, and timeless appeal in one of Islington’s most desirable addresses.THE LOCATIONNestled in the heart of Highbury, Leconfield Road, is a charming residence that effortlessly combines classic architectural elegance with the convenience of modern living. Perfectly situated in one of London’s most desirable neighborhoods, this property is surrounded by a wealth of local amenities, outstanding schools, and excellent transport links, making it a perfect choice for families, professionals, and anyone seeking a vibrant yet tranquil urban lifestyle.The property enjoys a prime location just a short stroll from Highbury & Islington Station, providing seamless access to the Victoria Line, London Overground, and National Rail services—ideal for commuters and city explorers alike. For those relying on local bus services, the nearest stop on Highbury New Park is only a five-minute walk away, offering frequent connections to central London and surrounding areas. The area is well-known for its strong sense of community and its abundance of conveniences, from charming independent boutiques and convenience stores to a selection of high-end shops and vibrant markets on nearby Upper Street.The culinary scene in this neighborhood is nothing short of exceptional, with a variety of options to suit every palate. Whether you’re enjoying a classic British meal at the renowned Highbury Barn Tavern, indulging in the finest artisanal cheeses and deli selections at La Fromagerie, or savoring a quiet coffee at one of the many cozy cafes, you’ll never run out of delightful dining experiences. For everyday needs, the local shops along Highbury Barn and Upper Street offer everything from fresh produce to unique gifts and essentials, ensuring residents have everything they need within easy reach.For families, the property is perfectly positioned near several Ofsted-rated schools that deliver outstanding education. Gillespie Primary School and St. Joan of Arc RC Primary School are highly regarded for their commitment to academic excellence and nurturing environments. Additionally, Highbury Fields School, a top-performing all-girls secondary school, is a short distance away and boasts an impressive reputation for preparing students for future success.With its enviable location, offers the perfect balance of city convenience and neighborhood charm. Whether you’re exploring the bustling shopping and dining scene on Upper Street, enjoying the green spaces of Highbury Fields, or taking advantage of the excellent transport links to explore the wider city, this property provides an unparalleled lifestyle opportunity in one of London’s most sought-after areas.Imminent interest is expected. Call us now to arrange a viewing. ‘Think…Property Inc’

This stunning five bedroom family house has been finished to high standard and provides ideal accommodation for a family. The former porch has been incorporated to create a welcoming entrance space with room for shoes, coats and umbrellas. The ground floor features an impressive double reception room with bespoke cabinetry, a modern conservatory, and contemporary kitchen leading to a welcoming dining room, creating an extremely versatile and stylish entertaining space. Another door leads from the spacious hallway to a useful utility room, downstairs WC, and on to a private office annexe with its own separate entrance, creating the perfect space for a home run business without any interruption to the main living space. Large windows throughout flood the property with natural light and a feeling of calm.The first floor benefits from a master bedroom suite with dressing room and ensuite bathroom, plus two further bedrooms both well equipped with built in storage, and a large family bathroom. A loft conversion has created a second floor with two more good sized rooms, currently used as a cinema room and gym, which could also be used as further bedrooms.The wide westerly facing private garden is laid to lawn, bordered by shrubs and trees, and features a decked veranda plus built in outdoor seating area providing a multitude of options for alfresco dining. To the front of the property is off street parking for two cars, in addition to the unrestricted parking available on the road. Kingston Vale is well placed for Richmond Park, Kingston, Putney and Wimbledon all offering a wide variety of excellent schools, restaurants and cafes. There are several golf courses in the immediate vicinity. The A3 is easily accessible and provides a fast route in and out of central London and to the M25 for access to Heathrow and Gatwick airports.

This impressive detached 1920s period home offers 2,731 square feet of elegant living space, combining Art Deco character with superb scope for further enhancement. Arranged over two floors, the property provides four generous bedrooms and two bathrooms on the upper level, ideal for comfortable family living.On the ground floor, a welcoming entrance hall leads to two spacious reception rooms, a bright kitchen and dining area, and a convenient guest WC. The layout offers an excellent sense of flow and the versatility to adapt to modern lifestyles.Outside, the property features a large private rear garden, a wonderful setting for entertaining or family time. At the end of the garden there is a flexible detached studio, perfect for use as a home office, gym or creative workspace.There is huge potential to extend the property at the rear, into the loft and into the existing garage, subject to planning permission.Perfectly positioned in a sought after location, this rare detached home will be an ideal choice for a growing family.Dobree Avenue is a peaceful residential street located in the heart of NW10, popular with buyers looking for more space while still being within easy reach of vibrant local amenities. The area offers a strong sense of community, leafy surroundings, and characterful homes that appeal to families and professionals alike.Location:Most residents gravitate towards the lively scenes on College Road and Chamberlayne Road, where you’ll find a great mix of independent shops, cafés, and restaurants. Popular local spots include The Chamberlayne Pub, Morty & Bob’s Café, and a host of other neighbourhood favourites. For a broader choice of dining and nightlife, Queens Park and Salusbury Road are only a short distance away, offering everything from artisan bakeries to stylish bars.Transport links are another advantage of the location. Kensal Rise Overground Station is within easy reach, providing quick connections across London, while those preferring the Underground can head to Willesden Green Station on the Jubilee Line for direct access to the West End and Canary Wharf.Local amenities are also strong. Willesden Sports Centre offers excellent fitness and leisure facilities, and King Edward VII Park provides open green space ideal for outdoor activities and family time.Overall, Dobree Avenue represents excellent value for money compared to neighbouring Queens Park and Kensal Rise, allowing buyers to enjoy more space without compromising on access to the amenities and lifestyle these popular areas offer.BrochuresWeb Details

There is even the potential to extend on this lovely residence, subject to planning permission.This residence is approached by it’s own-driveway with private off street parking for the family cars. It further comprises of a spacious and welcoming entrance hall giving access to a downstairs guest WC, a beautiful kitchen and the other ground floor rooms. Near the entrance we have a lovely room which can been used as a TV room, a lounge, a guest bedroom, or a study and is very versatile depending on the owner’s needs. Located off the hall is also a beautifully spacious reception room, leading to a bright and grand conservatory, which is ideal as a spacious and elegant dining area with plenty of additional family space around, and offering views over the private secluded mature garden. This area offers further access to the reception and also links to the spacious modern fitted kitchen/dining room and leads to the utility room with side access to the garden. To the first floor you will find 4 very well-proportioned bedrooms one of which is a huge master bedroom with a luxurious en-suite. There is also a luxury modern fitted family bathroom on this floor. One of the bedrooms is currently being used as smart home office and study. To the second floor are a further 2 charming and characterful guest bedrooms one with a further en-suite. Externally there is a well-tended mature rear garden with shrubs, bushes, trees, a large garden shed and a patio for alfresco dining and outdoor entertaining. The property is situated near local shops, and Brent Cross Shopping is easily accessible. All the facilities of the West End shops, restaurants and theatres are easily reached from here as well. Ideally situated for many amenities, including some great parks and transport links with access to North Circular Road, the A1 and M1 all close by. For tube and train travel Hendon Central Underground Station is 0.5 miles away. This lovely property is ideal for a family too with great Schools moments away, Including Private, Grammar and Faith Schools to choose from and places of worship in walking distance. There is also potential to extend subject to the necessary planning consents.Council Tax: GWARNING: Never transfer funds to a property owner before viewing the property in person. description: A beautifully reimagined five-bedroom home on Durham Road offers 2,004 sq ft (186.2 m²) of meticulously curated living space. Thoughtfully transformed by renowned architects Studio Mills, this home embraces harmony and function, with a palette of calming natural materials and an abundance of natural light throughout. This is a flowing contemporary family home that was fully furbished in 2024 and effortlessly balances heritage charm with modern design.At the heart of the ground floor is a bespoke kitchen and dining space, created by removing a dated rear extension and introducing a glass roof side return. The result is a wide, light-filled open-plan kitchen with a stunning Calacatta Viola marble island, handmade oak cabinetry by Goldfinch, and seamless access to the landscaped West-facing garden through Maxlight sliding doors. A discreet oak-clad pantry, underfloor heating, integrated air-con, Neff and Liebherr appliances complete the space, ideal for entertaining and family life.Connected via pocket doors, the adjoining music room and lounge offer a more intimate environment, reconfigured to encourage circulation and togetherness, while maintaining zones for privacy and calm.Upstairs, the home provides five well-proportioned bedrooms, including two with luxurious en suites complete with underfloor heating. A purpose-built laundry cupboard on the first floor adds everyday practicality, while rooflights and thoughtfully placed glazing ensure every corridor and corner is bright and uplifting.The Owners Love "The house was architecturally designed with real attention to detail - it's filled with natural light all day long. Every room feels bright, airy, and a genuinely lovely place to be" "The big marble-topped kitchen island is the heart of the home. It’s where everything happens - family breakfasts, kids doing homework, and evening drinks with friends" "Our neighbours are wonderful, and the street itself is so peaceful. At the same time, it's just a short stroll to the tube, so getting into town is quick and easy"We’ve NoticedThe property is equipped with a Worcester boiler, Corston Architectural switches and sockets, Megaflo system, and a Google Nest heating and smoke alarm setup, ensuring efficient and responsive climate control throughout the year.From the striking Sho Sugi Ban cladding on the full-width loft extension to the bronze-glass en-suite in the top floor bedroom, this home combines timeless materials with forward-thinking spatial planning.BrochuresParticulars

location: Viceroy Court, Prince Albert Road, London NW8
description: A beautifully presented 3-bedroom, 3-bathroom flat in the prestigious Viceroy Court has come on the market for the first time in 10 years. Situated on the fourth floor with lifts, this property enjoys high-quality finishes throughout.The flat comprises three spacious double bedrooms, two of which are en-suite, plus a guest bathroom. A large double front-facing reception room opens onto a balcony, providing a bright, airy space perfect for entertaining. A separate modern kitchen is fully fitted, and the property benefits from air conditioning, wooden flooring, and ample natural light throughout.Viceroy Court offers 24-hour uniformed porter service, recently refurbished communal areas, and a communal roof garden with panoramic views of the London skyline. Residents also enjoy off-road forecourt parking and manicured gardens.The location is exceptional, directly opposite Regent's Park and the Regent's Park Canal, yet just minutes from the West End. St John's Wood High Street, Primrose Hill, and Regent's Park Road offer a range of boutiques, cafes, restaurants, and cultural amenities. Lord's Cricket Ground, Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, Hampstead Theatre, and leading private hospitals are all within easy reach, alongside some of London's most sought-after schools.Combining park-side tranquillity with city convenience, this flat is ideal for families, professionals, or anyone seeking a refined London lifestyle.Owners' Questionnaire:-What attracted you to this specific house and location?The area is wonderfully green and feels peaceful, yet it's very close to central London. The flat itself is generously sized, and the porter service is exceptional - truly hotel-like. Having lived in other blocks on Prince Albert Road in the past, we can confidently say that Viceroy Court is the best by far.What are the neighbours like?Everyone here is friendly, and many residents have lived in the building for 20+ years. The community spirit is strong, with summer and Christmas gatherings organised by the building. There are also a few famous residents that live here.Memorable/remarkable event?There are countless special moments, but one highlight is enjoying the views and fireworks from the roof garden on New Year's Eve, truly unforgettable.How long have you owned your property?Since the summer of 2016.Who do you think would be the ideal next owner?This home would suit a young couple starting a family, an older couple, or even parents with children at various stages or even as a pied de Terre. What can you tell us about the history of the property and how it has been adapted by previous owners?Viceroy Court has a rich history. During World War II, it was used by the RAF, and it became a luxury apartment block in the 1930s, offering residents stunning views of Regent's Park and the London skyline along with modern amenities. The previous owners were doctors who used the flat as a holiday home; they maintained it beautifully.Why have you decided to move on?We now spend very little time in the UK, only a few days each year. It's a shame, because if we lived here full-time, we would keep it. Maintaining such a large property for occasional visits no longer makes sense, although we have ensured it is well cared for and cleaned regularly while away.How convenient is it for commuting from here?The location is extremely convenient. You can easily walk to multiple parks, enjoy strolls along the canal, and access shops, restaurants, supermarkets, and cinemas in St John's Wood, Marylebone, Primrose Hill, Swiss Cottage, Maida Vale, and Baker Street.There's a bus stop right outside the building with routes to Baker Street, Oxford Street, and Hyde Park. It's about a 20-minute drive to the City of London, or roughly 25 minutes by tube during the morning commute. Both nearby stations are within a comfortable walking distance.Tenure: Share of Freehold (960 years)Ground Rent: £0 per yearBrochuresBrochure

A more recently built two-story annexe linked to the main house provides a study with feature French windows, a further bedroom/playroom and shower room, a wine cellar, a spacious garage and a store room. features: Located in the Heart of Angel
description: A rare opportunity to acquire this stunning Grade II listed Georgian house, offering elegant period features and generous living space across four floors. This beautifully preserved three-bedroom, two-bathroom home is positioned on the sought-after Charlton Place in the heart of Islington. Accessed via a charming ground floor entrance, the home opens into a double reception room boasting high ceilings, original detailing, and a Juliette balcony overlooking the south-facing garden—ideal for entertaining or relaxing in style. A half-landing leads directly out to the garden, seamlessly connecting the indoors with the outdoors. On the lower ground floor, a bespoke kitchen sits at the front of the property, opening to a private front patio and traditional vault, perfect for storage. To the rear, a cosy seating area offers a tranquil space with further direct access to the garden. The first floor hosts a spacious principal bedroom and a large, stylish four-piece family bathroom. Two additional well-proportioned double bedrooms are found on the second floor, one of which benefits from an en-suite shower room. With an abundance of natural light, characterful features, and a superb south-facing garden, this is a truly special Georgian home in one of Islington’s most desirable streets.Charlton Place is a picturesque, tree-lined street set moments from the vibrant heart of Angel and Upper Street. This peaceful enclave offers a quintessential Islington lifestyle—blending period charm with modern convenience. Residents enjoy easy access to an array of independent boutiques, acclaimed restaurants, cafés, and gastro pubs on Upper Street. Just around the corner is the renowned Camden Passage, famous for its antique market, artisan shops, and hidden coffee spots. Everyday essentials are easily found nearby, with supermarkets, gyms, and green spaces such as Islington Green and Regent’s Canal all within walking distance. Charlton Place benefits from excellent transport connections. Angel Underground Station (Northern Line) is within close proximity, offering fast access to the City and West End. Highbury & Islington Station (Victoria Line, Overground, and National Rail) is also within easy reach. Multiple bus routes run along Upper Street, providing swift connections across London, while cycle-friendly infrastructure makes commuting by bike a convenient option.Selling your property in Charlton Place? features: Close to Blackheath Standard, Greenwich Park, and the Heath
description: High Ceilings, A Huge Kitchen And 100ft Of Private South Facing Garden. Turn left and you’re on the Heath, turn right and you’re in the village – this is Blackheath living at its best, with four floors of warmth, character and proper family-sized space.This impressive Victorian semi-detached has four floors that flow in a way that makes everyday life feel easy – with spaces to come together, spread out or simply step outside for a bit of sun.Stone steps lead up to the raised ground floor, where you’ll find a light and bright bay-fronted family room at the front and a cosy reception room at the rear. French doors opening to the balcony overlooking the back garden – a perfect suntrap for morning coffee or an evening glass of wine. There’s a wood burner here for the colder months, and tucked below the balcony you’ll find a handy space to store your logs.Downstairs, the 25m kitchen-diner is where everyone gathers – with space for 20+ party guests, a Smeg range, shaker cabinetry and a traditional butler sink. This is where Christmas for 16 happens without a hitch, or BBQs spill out into the garden with ease. This floor even has a guest bathroom with shower, and it's been future-proofed with plumbing for underfloor heating, with a manifold already in place.Upstairs, the first floor holds two generous double bedrooms and a separate w/c. The main front bedroom gets lovely south-facing light and already has plumbing in place should you want to add an en-suite.The top floor has three further bedrooms – all light, thoughtfully designed and ready for real family life. One room features a secret climbing ladder hidden behind shelves, another has Andrew Martin wallpaper, and built-in furniture was added just last year. There’s storage in all the right places, and the family bathroom is finished with Neptune cabinetry, marble tiles and C.P. Hart fittings.The 100ft south-facing garden has something for everyone. There are raised beds, apple trees, a large lawn and a proper climbing frame with tower, slide and swing. Mature trees offer that sweet spot between light and shade, while side access makes coming back from a bike ride easier. There's a large shed at the rear and more storage tucked away beneath the balcony steps.The house was fully rewired and re-plumbed in 2020, with a Nest heating system zoned across all three levels. The loft - accessed via a retractable ladder on the landing - is fully boarded and insulated - offering excellent storage and housing the central heating system. The front reception room is incredibly flexible - it’s currently being used as a family space but it would be ideal as a playroom, home office or even a second dining area. The location is hard to beat. Turn left and you’re on the Heath with Greenwich Park just beyond. Turn right and you're in the heart of the village, with old-school specialists like the butcher, grocer and hardware shop sitting alongside cafés, a brewery and M&S.The street itself is friendly and neighbourly – the kind where people stop to chat, put the bins out when you're away, and host garden drinks in the summer.You’re only a 10-minute walk to Westcombe Park station, 15 minutes to Blackheath, with regular buses to North Greenwich (Jubilee Line), Greenwich (DLR & Overground). The City’s just 35 minutes away, and West End stations like Charing Cross or Victoria can be reached in about 45 minutes.It’s a substantial family home with real heart, proper space and a layout that works. And all this in a location that gives you the best of Blackheath – the open Heath to your left, the village to your right, and a real sense of community all around you.Four floors of Victorian charm, big family spaces and a 100ft south-facing garden25m sociable shaker kitchen with butler sink, Smeg range and space for epic partiesTwo reception rooms with a balcony and a cosy log burner for winter eveningsFive bedrooms with built-in storage, designer touches and a secret climbing ladderEPC Rating: D

description: Riverstone Bishops Avenue is a residence of one, two, three-bedroom and Penthouse apartments in area of the capital that enjoys the best of both worlds. With easy access into central London, Riverstone Bishops Avenue is set amongst mature trees and richly landscaped gardens, showcasing Arts and Crafts inspired design in an ancient woodland setting Just a stone's throw from Hampstead Heath, it's a perfect location to enjoy Kenwood House, heathland walks, swimming ponds, and a mass of green open space. Each apartment has been designed to ensure it feels like home the moment you step through your front door. A spacious place to relax and enjoy time with family and friends, with floor-to-ceiling windows and contemporary finishes throughout. Every apartment has its own private terrace to enjoy, so you can feel the connection with nature throughout the seasons. Clean lines and classically neutral decor make the perfect backdrop for your treasured belongings. Gardens play a key role in health and wellbeing and at Riverstone, we put outdoor space at the centre of our designs. Our cosy, welcoming bar is the perfect spot to enjoy a drink, perhaps before watching a film in the private cinema, and our restaurant offers a fresh, delicious menu in friendly surroundings. features: Suitable for those with art collections
description: This three-bedroom apartment, with interiors designed by murphy / davé architects, uses innovative lighting throughout and features smoked solid oak wood, Italian limestone and other natural materials. It’s ideal for those looking for a certain lifestyle, as it provides high specification living with plenty of room to entertain, as well as tranquil spaces to enjoy quiet downtime. Living space: From the private entrance hall and gallery, the apartment opens up into a spectacular double height reception area. Stepping down into the space there’s a study to one side hidden behind a full height, sliding door clad in red hide, as well as a gym/yoga room. The dining area sits beneath double-height glazing which opens on to cedar decking, while the kitchen and utility room features a hand-finished Bulthaup-designed kitchen with appliances by Miele and Neff. An additional photo is included of the media room with CGI images of the kitchen.The media room includes blackout shutters with doors that open on to the garden. Both the gallery and the media room are ideal for entertaining. Bedrooms: The air-conditioned principal bedroom is a calm and relaxing Zen-like space with a luxurious leather floor, and is surrounded on two sides by floor to ceiling windows which float above the garden’s reflecting pool. There’s also a walk-through dressing room, a substantial ensuite bathroom with Italian limestone slab work and fittings by Vola, plus a separate walk-in shower. The second bedroom has an ensuite bathroom as well as an adjacent multi-function room. The third bedroom is accessed from the second bedroom. There is plenty of opportunity to configure the space to suit your needs, if a third double bedroom is required with its own entrance. The media room could be converted into an additional bedroom.Outside space: The low maintenance Japanese-style south-facing garden wraps around two sides of the apartment and includes a reflecting pool and a cedar wood deck, as well as mature olive trees, bamboo and Mediterranean palms. An Italian limestone patio is perfect for entertaining and BBQs, and there’s room for a sauna or ice bath. There is also designated off-street parking. The local area: The apartment is one of eight apartments in Hotham Hall, which is a short walk to vibrant Lower Richmond Road which has coffee shops, a greengrocer’s, a popular local butcher, The Parson’s Nose, as well as an art gallery. Putney High Street is close by too, and trains from Putney train station to central London take just 18 minutes. East Putney and Putney Bridge stations on the District line are within walking distance. The apartment benefits from a high-end alarm system, electric shutters and a secure building, so it’s the ideal property for those who spend time overseas for work or pleasure.

features: Quite street moments from Westbourne Grove
description: On a quiet residential street in Notting Hill, this two-bedroom home reflects the refined sensibility of designer and architect Samuel Chisholm. Thoughtfully reimagined for modern living, its interiors balance crafted detail with a calm sense of space. The entrance opens to a semi-open kitchen and dining area, where birch ply shutters draw back to welcome the southern light. Terrazzo worktops and a polished copper splashback define the space - tactile and practical, with a subtle play of reflection that broadens its proportions. Clerestory windows and a skylight above a reading nook add soft, diffuse light throughout the day. A sculptural staircase, inspired by Swiss architect Adolphe Appia, runs through the heart of the home. Its illuminated balustrade and Dinesen Douglas fir treads bring warmth and rhythm to the interior. On the first floor, the reception room feels both uplifting and grounded, its dual-aspect glazing framing views of neighbouring gardens. In summer, the double doors open to extend the living space into the leafy backdrop beyond. The principal bedroom sits beneath the eaves, a serene retreat with a bright, alpine character. Blonde timber panelling, cork floors and a compact log burner create a natural, restorative mood. A set of concertina doors reveals the en-suite bathroom - its bath positioned beneath a dormer window and a milled brass panel that catches the light in soft, golden tones. On the lower level, a versatile guest bedroom can also serve as a study or studio. Pale tones and clean lines maintain the sense of continuity, while shutters connect the room to a second bathroom finished in dusty pink micro-cement with brushed brass fittings. Artesian Road sits quietly just off Ledbury Road - a calm, residential street at the heart of Notting Hill. Around the corner are some of the area's most celebrated names, from Ottolenghi and The Ledbury to the wellness hubs of FaceGym and Caudalie Boutique Spa. A short stroll brings you to the energy of Westbourne Grove and Portobello Road, where independent galleries, vintage boutiques and eateries line the streets. Everyday essentials can be found at Notting Hill Fish and Meat Shop, while Core by Clare Smyth offers an exceptional fine dining experience just moments away. With the Underground within easy reach, central London feels close, yet life here retains a distinctly local rhythm.BrochuresBrochure 1

features: Landscaped rear garden with self-contained home office
0.5 miles to Greenwich Mainline and DLR Station
description: A striking Grade II-listed Georgian townhouse located on the desirable Hyde Vale in the heart of West Greenwich. The house has been refurbished to create a stylish and modern home providing exceptional living space and generous bedrooms on the upper floors. The kitchen is centrally located on the raised ground floor with ample worktop and storage space with a kitchen island/breakfast bar. The dining room is to the rear, perfect for families and entertaining, with French doors leading out onto the rear garden and patio area. The lower ground floor features a splendid double reception room with bespoke fitted cabinetry and a utility room with guest WC to the rear while french doors provide access at both the front and rear. The first floor comprises of two generous bedrooms with the front room currently being utilised as a reception room with large sash windows and a period style fireplace. The rear bedroom features fitted wardrobes and lovely views into the garden. The top floor features a large bedroom to the front and a spacious bathroom suite to the rear complete with a stand-alone bathtub and a walk in shower. There is also a lovely seating area on the landing between both bedroom levels and a dressing room at the top of the staircase. The rear garden has been meticulously landscaped to create a stunning leafy oasis with a patio area perfect for alfresco dining. The garden leads up past a tranquil water feature to a self-contained home office/studio complete with a guest WC and a south-facing private terrace which is perfect for working from home away from the main house or a separate guest space. This wonderful home is incredibly unique and provides the opportunity to purchase a historic home on arguably West Greenwich's most sought after location.SituationHyde Vale is a sweeping road in West Greenwich which has recently been closed to through traffic at peak times creating a wonderfully quiet residential road. All of Greenwich's shops, bars and restaurants are within easy reach as are all the historical places of interest such as the National Maritime Museum, Royal Observatory and the Royal Naval College. It's a short walk from both Cutty Sark and Greenwich DLR stations as well as the Mainline Rail Station at Greenwich giving fast and convenient access into Canary Wharf and the City. Property Ref Number: HAM-59481

"Riverstone Fulham is situated in a prime location on the north bank of the River Thames. Our generous riverside terrace and gardens make the most of this exceptional riverside location. Ideal for a morning or evening stroll, the popular Thames Path runs alongside Riverstone Fulham and the Hurlingham Club is a short distance away. The open plan design of the apartments and floor-to-ceiling windows create a light and spacious feel to echo the riverside location. With great storage space and elegant neutral decor and finishing, the spaces at Riverstone Fulham offer a blank canvas for you to make your own and really call home. Every apartment has its own private terrace to enjoy, so you can feel the connection with nature throughout the seasons. Every detail has been considered so you can live in ultimate style and comfort. Working with RHS Chelsea Flower Show award-winner Andy Sturgeon, the landscaped areas at Riverstone Fulham form a series of connected green promenades, filled with colourful, textural and scented plants. Designed to be enjoyed all year round, these are places in which you and your visitors can be active - or simply relax and enjoy the surroundings. An abundant herb garden has been created for your enjoyment offering the opportunity to nurture fresh produce. The health and wellbeing, social and dining environments at Riverstone Fulham have been created to be an extension of your home, ready for you to use, as and when you choose, on your own, or with friends and family. Our cosy, welcoming bar is the perfect spot to enjoy a drink, perhaps before watching a film in the private cinema, and our restaurant offers a fresh, delicious menu in a riverside setting. Gym * Spa * Restaurant * Espresso Bar * Residents' Lounge * Private Cinema * Exercise Studio * Landscaped Gardens * Hair and Nail Salon Other fees apply. Prices are correct for selected apartments. description: Our brand new marketing suite on Kensington High Street is now open for viewings. Introducing a unique collection of exquisite apartments in an exclusive new Kensington landmark. This exceptional residential opportunity in London's Royal Borough presents 100 Kensington, featuring 276 apartments elegantly distributed across four distinguished buildings. The first release, The Oria, is a striking 29-storey tower offering an exclusive collection comprising 129 studios, one, two and three bedroom apartments available to purchase off-plan. The Oria will be the tallest residential building in the Royal Borough of Kensington & Chelsea, offering unparalleled views of this storied neighbourhood and the city beyond. World Class Amenities The podium level features three floors above ground with an impressive double-height atrium and dedicated concierge service available 12 hours daily, 7 days a week. Residents can enjoy a sophisticated residents' lounge for relaxation and socialising, beautifully landscaped gardens showcasing sculptural art by acclaimed artist David Harbour, and a private cinema with versatile multi-use games and event spaces. The ground level offers comprehensive wellness facilities including a tranquil 20-metre swimming pool, state-of-the-art fully equipped gym, and a dedicated fitness studio for classes and personal training. Prime Location & Connectivity 100 Kensington enjoys an enviable position adjacent to the Cromwell Road, providing direct access to central London with Buckingham Palace to the East and Heathrow Airport to the West. The development is surrounded by multiple Tube stations including West Kensington, High Street Kensington, Kensington Olympia and Earls Court, all within a short walking distance. For luxury shopping enthusiasts, 100 Kensington is ideally situated near High Street Kensington, Westfield London, and Knightsbridge. Families will appreciate the proximity to some of the UK's highest-rated private and state schools and colleges. Enquire now to schedule your private viewing. *CGIs are for indicative purposes only.

"Riverstone Fulham is situated in a prime location on the north bank of the River Thames. Our generous riverside terrace and gardens make the most of this exceptional riverside location. Ideal for a morning or evening stroll, the popular Thames Path runs alongside Riverstone Fulham and the Hurlingham Club is a short distance away. The open plan design of the apartments and floor-to-ceiling windows create a light and spacious feel to echo the riverside location. With great storage space and elegant neutral decor and finishing, the spaces at Riverstone Fulham offer a blank canvas for you to make your own and really call home. Every apartment has its own private terrace to enjoy, so you can feel the connection with nature throughout the seasons. Every detail has been considered so you can live in ultimate style and comfort. Working with RHS Chelsea Flower Show award-winner Andy Sturgeon, the landscaped areas at Riverstone Fulham form a series of connected green promenades, filled with colourful, textural and scented plants. Designed to be enjoyed all year round, these are places in which you and your visitors can be active - or simply relax and enjoy the surroundings. An abundant herb garden has been created for your enjoyment offering the opportunity to nurture fresh produce. The health and wellbeing, social and dining environments at Riverstone Fulham have been created to be an extension of your home, ready for you to use, as and when you choose, on your own, or with friends and family. Our cosy, welcoming bar is the perfect spot to enjoy a drink, perhaps before watching a film in the private cinema, and our restaurant offers a fresh, delicious menu in a riverside setting. Gym * Spa * Restaurant * Espresso Bar * Residents' Lounge * Private Cinema * Exercise Studio * Landscaped Gardens * Hair and Nail Salon Other fees apply. Prices are correct for selected apartments. Photographs of show apartments are for guidance purposes only. All apartments are sold unfurnished."

Introducing a brand-new luxurious five-bedroom family home, offering an impressive 2,884 sq ft of contemporary living. This is a stunning, detached residence, meticulously crafted to a high standard spanning across three floors. Combining sleek architectural design with high-end finishes, this exceptional home has a perfect layout for both entertaining and everyday comfort. Set on a generous plot with a large in-and-out driveway and providing parking for several cars, the property features beautifully landscaped gardens and a South-East facing rear garden that enjoys sun throughout the day. On the second floor you can enjoy views of Wimbledon and Putney Commons.Ground Floor – Elegant Design Meets Functional Luxury.Step into a magnificent entrance hallway, showcasing a floating oak staircase spanning across three floors. With toughened glass balustrades, oak bannisters and a luminescent, sand painted feature wall surrounding a ceiling hung statement chandelier which can be admired at each level.The heart of the home is the open-plan kitchen, dining, and family area, a vast entertaining space with the opportunity to zone areas within the space. The bespoke handleless kitchen features cashmere units with anodised bronze trims, contrasting dark feature island with an oak overhung breakfast bar and drop down feature lighting. A hosting area with fluted windows, oxidised mirror panels and storage allows efficient access when dining inside or out. The kitchen has a hidden larder unit with folding doors to conceal and declutter the countertops, keeping appliances out of sight but readily accessible. Premium Bosch appliances are installed. Broad bi-fold doors open onto a porcelain patio and garden. Accessible through the kitchen is a concealed utility room allowing side access. This offers floor to ceiling storage, a second sink, with space for a stacked washer and dryer, all finished with Quartz worktops and splashbacks for a seamless look.The lounge area is partitioned with crittall doors and a beautifully housed double-sided bio-ethanol fireplace, creating a perfect balance of warmth and modern sophistication in both dining and lounge areas.Upper Floors – Tranquil Bedrooms & Spa Like Bathrooms.The property boasts five well-proportioned bedrooms, each beautifully finished with soft, luxurious neutral carpets and well considered designer lighting. Each bedroom features expansive wall-to-wall windows that flood the interior with natural light, creating a bright and airy atmosphere.The principal suite offers a stunning en-suite bathroom, while two further bathrooms; a family bathroom on the first floor and a luxury suite on the top floor, feature full-height tiling, illuminated niches, and modern feature walls, creating a boutique hotel ambience.Technology, Comfort & Craftsmanship.A wet underfloor heating system runs throughout the ground floor, zoned for comfort and efficiency, supported by a Vaillant system boiler and a 300L hot water cylinder discreetly housed in a dedicated plant room. Installed with smart lighting throughout, the home includes coffered ceilings with LED lighting, designer radiators, and recessed lighting in key spaces, all thoughtfully designed to enhance the mood and atmosphere. Exterior & Garden.Outside, the property makes a strong first impression with its double block-paved driveway, rendered and brick façade, and aluminium roof detailing.To the rear, the landscaped South-East facing garden offers a porcelain stone patio, turfed lawn, and rendered planter walls with a private seating area, ideal for outdoor entertaining. The rear of the house is no less impressive, maintaining the same bold architectural presence and visual appeal as the front.A purpose-built garden office/gym, clad in low-maintenance composite material, with wall to wall bifold doors provides the perfect space for remote work, fitness, or a serene indoor-outdoor living space within the garden.Location – Kingston Vale.The property is located in the popular Kingston Vale area, ideally positioned between Richmond Park and Wimbledon Common, offering easy access to beautiful open spaces and a great choice of outdoor activities.There are a number of local shops and amenities close by, as well as the well-regarded Robin Hood Primary School, making this a convenient spot for families.For commuters, the A3 is just moments away, providing direct routes into Central London, Surrey, and the M25. The nearby centres of Kingston upon Thames, Wimbledon Village, Richmond, and Putney all offer a wide range of shops, restaurants, and leisure facilities, ensuring everything you need is within easy reach.Kingston Vale offers a great blend of green surroundings and excellent transport links, making it a highly desirable place to call home.In Summary.This brand-new five-bedroom family home combines architectural flair, intelligent design, and exceptional craftsmanship. From the elegant interior detailing to the landscaped outdoor spaces, every element has been considered to create a home that is as practical as it is impressive. BrochuresParticulars

location: Rennie Court, 11 Upper Ground, London, SE1
description: A rare opportunity to acquire a beautifully presented, modern four-bedroom duplex apartment spanning nearly 2,300 sq ft over two floors in the heart of Southbank. This exceptional home features underfloor heating, a wine cellar, and two expansive private terraces with stunning views of the City skyline, including St Paul’s Cathedral. Residents enjoy a large secure underground parking space, 24-hour concierge service, and proximity to world-class cultural landmarks. EPC rating DOccupying the seventh and eighth floors, this stylish duplex offers a superb blend of generous living, entertaining, and private space—finished to a high modern standard.On the lower level, the welcoming entrance hallway leads to a sleek, contemporary kitchen/breakfast room with integrated appliances, a breakfast bar, and engineered wood flooring. Sliding glass doors open onto a large private terrace with breathtaking City views. Adjacent to the kitchen is a spacious and bright reception room with a feature fireplace, ideal for both relaxed living and entertaining. This level also includes a guest W.C., built-in storage, a large airing cupboard, and a wine cellar discreetly tucked off the hallway—perfect for collectors or those who love to entertain.Also on this floor is a generously sized guest bedroom with a stylish en-suite bathroom, including a walk-in rainfall shower, vanity with storage, heated towel rail, and W.C.The main terrace features built-in outdoor cooking facilities, ample counter space, and room for lounge and dining furniture—perfect for summer entertaining against the backdrop of the London skyline.Upstairs, the principal bedroom suite offers a spacious retreat with its own en-suite bathroom and a large walk-in dressing area. Two further double bedrooms each open directly onto the second private terrace, offering elevated views across the River Thames. One of the additional bedrooms features a walk-in wardrobe, while both accommodate king-size beds and further furnishings with ease.A stylish family bathroom with a full-size bath, overhead shower, heated towel rail, and vanity unit completes the upstairs space, along with a convenient utility room.Throughout the apartment, you’ll find underfloor heating and double glazing, ensuring year-round comfort and energy efficiency.Residents benefit from a large, secure underground parking space and access to excellent amenities within the adjacent five-star Sea Containers hotel, including bars, restaurants, a cinema, and optional spa/gym membership. Within the development itself are a Waitrose supermarket and a large pharmacy.Heating and hot water are included in the annual service charges.Leasehold - 99 years from 29 September 1975SERVICE CHARGE, GROUND RENT, COUNCIL TAXService Charge - £14,000 per annumGround Rent - £300 per annumCouncil Tax Band - FPARKINGSecure underground parking.UTILITIESElectricity – mains connectedGas – N/AWater – mains connected Heating – electric central heatingSewerage – mains connectedBroadband - Ultrafast BroadbandLocation:Set within Rennie Court—a striking Modernist development by renowned British architect Richard Seifert—this apartment enjoys a prime position between the iconic One Blackfriars and Southbank Tower. Just moments from the Thames, the location offers easy access to some of London’s most iconic destinations, including the London Eye, Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre, Tate Modern, Shakespeare’s Globe, Borough Market, and Tower Bridge. The vibrant Southbank area also boasts a wealth of excellent restaurants, bars, and shops.Directions:Rennie Court is located just 0.3 miles from Southwark Underground Station (Jubilee Line) and 0.3 miles from Blackfriars Underground Station (Circle & District Lines and Thameslink). There are regular bus routes and the Thames clipper riverboat by the Tate Modern close by.BrochuresWeb DetailsParticulars

A beautifully refurbished four double bedroom house in a quaint Bayswater mews DescriptionA fantastic four bedroom house with garage in one of Bayswater's highly desirable mews locations meters from the open spaces of Hyde Park and Kensington Gardens. Completely remodelled and refurbished throughout this contemporary 179 sq m (1,935 sq ft) four double bedroom mews house features bespoke joinery, feature lighting, luxury bathrooms and modern interiors while still retaining the charm of a traditional style mews house. The accommodation is arranged across four floors and offers generous and versatile living and entertaining space. The ground floor has a central hallway, a large double bedroom with en suite shower room, guest cloakroom and internal access to the integral garage. The feature open tread staircase leads up to the first floor with double doors opening out to the pretty private terrace. Completeing this floor there is the principle bedroom with en suite and built in storage, another double bedroom with built in storage and a family bathroom. The generous sized kitchen reception/dining room spans the the top floor and is filled with natural light from the two French windows. There is a lovely semi-open plan kitchen which features a breakfast bar and Siemens integrated appliances. The lower ground is home to the second reception room which can be utilised in many ways depending on the need of the buyer. The fourth double bedroom with en suite bathroom and the handy separate utility room.LocationThis property is well-connected by several transport options, including: - Lancaster Gate (Central Line) - 0.3 miles - Paddington (Bakerloo, Circle, District, Hammersmith & City, Elizabeth Line, National Rail, Heathrow Express) - 0.4 miles - Bayswater (Circle and District Line) - 0.7 milesSquare Footage: 1,934 sq ft BrochuresWeb DetailsParticulars

Reflecting inspired architectural design, all apartments are light-filled and feature at least one spacious private terrace – your panoramic stage from which to admire London’s famed skyline, from St Paul’s to the Shard.Bankside is a vibrant area renowned for its extensive urban greenery. The Bankside Urban Forest, into which Triptych Bankside seamlessly integrates, has revitalized historic streets with lush plantings. This initiative has added over 250 trees and boosted green coverage by 1,000m². Residents enjoy access to numerous other green spaces, including Mint Street Park and Red Cross Gardens. For a unique blend of nature and riverside views, the Tate Moderngardens offer an ideal spot, often featuring live music and diverse food options.A new mixed-use development designed by internationally acclaimed London architects Squire & Partners, Triptych Bankside is a luxury architectural destination. A bold development with character, every home has outside space and the undulating curves of the façade create a new architectural narrative. The overall layout of the site has been meticulously planned to positively contribute to the local townscape and sense of place. This creates a seamless integration, with varied spaces to be enjoyed by residents and visitors alike.Residents can enjoy an uncompromising selection of five-star style services and private amenities, ensuring holistic wellness of both body and mind. Benefit from bespoke service at the 24-hour concierge, unwind in the cinema and games rooms, or let your creative spirit soar in the private lounge and workspace.Residences at Triptych Bankside combine exacting design, unrivalled craftsmanship, technology and specification to stunning effect. Reach home and feel an innate sense of belonging. Reflecting inspired architectural design, all apartments are light-filled and feature at least one spacious private terrace – your panoramic stage from which to admire London’s famed skyline, from St Paul’s to the Shard.Home to its own art school Cultural facility, Triptych Bankside welcomes you to join in its celebration of the arts, cultural, and social scene. Immerse yourself in the rich history of this thriving area, filled with world-renowned landmarks and attractions. The illustrious Tate Modern Gallery and the iconic home of Shakespeare’s Globe Theatre are literally on your doorstep. Also, numerous galleries in the immediate vicinity are bound to inspire your artistic spirit. Homes at Triptych Bankside are complemented by communal gardens, Art Academy and several boutique retailers. The relaxed yet welcoming atmosphere extends the stimulating cultural destination of Southwark with retailers including: The Font, a rock climbing and fitness gym; Solo60, an exclusive micro-gym; Nagare Café, a Japanese coffee shop; and Uncommon London, a gourmet deli and café.Art Academy London’s new campus is set over two floors with teaching facilities including nine studios and an auditorium. Designed by architects Squire & Partners the campus also includes specialist workshop spaces for local artists and communities as well as a 2,000 sq. ft. onsite gallery which is open to the public.A complete urban quarter, Triptych Bankside offers a variety of high-end retail and food destinations handpicked for their individuality and originality. Completed by a splendid wine bar, you never have to go far to indulge in exquisite delights. Venture out, and you will find surrounding Bankside is a gastronomic district in its own right, host to an exotic mix of food and drink experiences within easy reach. With the much loved Borough Market – London’s famous organic food market –only seven minutes on foot, look forward to being taken on a colourful journey of the senses.Within walking distance to significant landmarks, destinations, and public transport, the city’s pulse is continually at your fingertips. You can relish the scenic route into the City of London via the famous Millennium Bridge, or enjoy a leisurely stroll around the iconic South Bank adjacent to the historic River Thames. Alternatively, key transport hubs are within easy walking distance and offer excellent connectivity via bus, London Underground or National Rail. Centrally located, living at Triptych Bankside allows you to indulge in all the benefits this fabulous quarter presents.DisclaimerFelicity J Lord Estate Agents also offer a professional, ARLA accredited Lettings and Management Service. location: Old Brompton Road, South Kensington, London
description: Positioned on the top floor (with lift) of one of South Kensington’s most admired red-brick mansion buildings, this light-filled penthouse combines period proportions with a thoughtful modern layout. Built between 1901 and 1904 on the site of Coleherne House, Coleherne Court is known for its handsome architecture, 24-hour porter service, and access to one of the finest and largest private gardens in the area. The apartment was recently remodelled to create a larger and more open reception area, which leads out to a balcony through a set of French doors. The living space flows naturally into the adjoining dining room, which enjoys a leafy outlook, itself leading onto the kitchen, which is finished with marble worktops and quality appliances. The principal bedroom has a walk-in dressing room and an en suite shower room. Two further bedrooms are both fitted with built-in wardrobes and share a second shower room and a guest powder room. Wooden floors run through the main living spaces, with soft carpet underfoot in the bedrooms. Residents have full use of the beautifully kept communal gardens and secure bike storage. Coleherne Court sits in a particularly well-connected part of South Kensington, with Old Brompton Road and Earl’s Court moments away. The neighbourhood is known for its garden squares and village atmosphere, home to independent cafés, bakeries, and boutiques alongside classic local institutions such as The Troubadour. Bousefield, the renowned local state school is also situated next door on The Little Boltons. The open green spaces of Kensington Gardens, Hyde Park and Holland Park are within easy reach, as are world-renowned museums including the V&A and the Natural History Museum.

An additional formal living area and a library benefit from generous ceiling heights and abundance of natural light.The master bedroom includes ample storage and a luxurious en-suite bathroom. Two further double bedrooms are located on the top floor, sharing a well-appointed bathroom. A fourth double bedroom is situated on the lower ground floor with a seperate family bathroom, and enjoys the advantage of separate access. This home combines stylish interiors with exceptional outdoor spaces, making it a rare find in the heart of Chelsea.Located moments from the luxury boutiques and world-class restaurants of Kings Road, Sydney Street is well-positioned for excellent local schools, including the renowned French Lycée. The Natural History Museum and Victoria & Albert Museum are just a ten-minute walk away. Sloane Square and South Kensington stations (Piccadilly, District, and Circle Lines) are nearby, ensuring easy travel, while Victoria Station offers access to the national rail network and international airports.Reference number: ISL260138.BrochuresBrochure 1

Grade II listed semi-detached, four-bedroom home overlooking Albion Square. DescriptionSavills are delighted to offer on to the market this exceptional Grade II listed semi-detached family home overlooking one of Hackney's most desirable garden squares. The house is in beautiful condition throughout and benefits from over 2000 sq ft of living accommodation with many period features, including ceiling mouldings, wood shutters, high skirting boards, large sash windows and original fireplaces.The main entrance is accessed on the raised ground floor into the hallway, which has a utility room, cloakroom, and a door down to the garden. There is a large double reception room with sash windows overlooking the garden square to the front and garden to the rear, with fireplaces and a double door to separate the rooms.On the first floor are three bedrooms, built-in wardrobes and a modern fitted bathroom.On the lower ground floor is an open-plan kitchen/dining room with a bespoke fitted kitchen island, cabinetry cupboards and wood flooring throughout. There is a working wood burner and double glass doors opening onto the private rear garden. You'll also find a fourth bedroom with an en-suite shower room and an additional shower room to the front with further storage located under the main stairs.There is a generous 62 ft. landscaped rear garden with a patio area and built-in-side access which is used for storage of bikes etc.LocationSir William Middleton commissioned the building of Albion Square in 1844. Pevsner has described the design of the square as "clearly influenced by the work of the celebrated landscape designer and writer, John Claudius Loudon". The central garden was saved from demolition and remains an ornamental garden with symmetrical beds, grass and trees and a picturesque garden pavilion. Nearby are plenty of local amenities, including cafes, restaurants and pubs. The green open spaces of London Fields can be found 0.6 miles away, along with Broadway Market. There are numerous transport links nearby, including Haggerston Station (0.3 miles) and Dalston Junction (0.6 miles) along with many buses into the City.All times and distances are approximate.Square Footage: 2,050 sq ft BrochuresWeb Details

Nearly 120 years after its foundation stone was laid, Hampstead Garden Suburb remains true to Dame Henrietta Barnett's original ideals - a refuge from the hustle and bustle of a thriving city, yet just a short commute from central London. Designed by prominent architects Raymond Unwin and Barry Parker, Heath Close exemplifies the Arts and Crafts movement, with homes featuring high quality craftsmanship, harmonious proportions, and thoughtful integration with the natural landscape. Awarded Grade II listed status by Historic England in the late 1990s, Heath Close is set directly opposite the 80 acres of wild, open spaces which form the Hampstead Heath extension. Occupying a coveted end of close position behind vehicular gates, the property provides ample off-street parking for 3-4 cars, a stand-alone garage and mature gardens to the front and rear. Offering flexible living accommodation arranged over three floors, including spacious entertaining areas and a sensational principal bedroom suite, featuring vaulted ceilings extending to 3.6m in height, a full en-suite bathroom and dressing room. Heath Close is enviably located just over half a mile from Golders Green Underground Station (Northern Line), just under one mile from the cafes, bakeries and restaurants of Temple Fortune and is superbly positioned for an array of local independent schools, including The King Alfred's, UCS, Highgate, Channing and South Hampstead.BrochuresBrochure

location: Leamington Road Villas, Notting Hill, United Kingdom
description: Occupying the lower and ground floors of a handsome Victorian villa, this three-bedroom apartment was once a 19th-century vicarage, offering expansive floor space, soaring ceiling heights and period character rarely found elsewhere. The home sits in the Aldridge Road and Westbourne Conservation Area, a collection of elegant nineteenth-century streets developed during the westward expansion of London’s underground network. Many of the original façades remain intact, lending the area its distinctive stucco-fronted villas and leafy pavements. The ground floor centres around a large reception and dining room, anchored by a period fireplace and a beautiful bay window that floods the space with light. Original shutters frame the tall sash windows, and wooden floors run throughout, enhancing the sense of warmth and continuity. The room flows naturally into a well-designed family kitchen fitted with an Aga cooker and a generous island that doubles as a breakfast table. The layout encourages conversation while cooking, and doors open directly onto the surrounding patio, perfect for entertaining on warmer days. A library and a guest bedroom can also be found on this level, creating flexible space for work or guests. A study and a guest powder room complete this floor. On the lower ground floor, the principal bedroom suite has a large walk-in wardrobe and an elegant ensuite bathroom with a freestanding bath. A second bedroom also enjoys its own ensuite, while the third bedroom has an adjoining study. A further powder room completes this floor. There are also two very large walk-in storage areas outside, one of which could easily be converted into a sauna or children’s playroom. An indoor–outdoor gym sits just off the patio, providing a unique space for year-round fitness and wellbeing. The apartment is surrounded by a private patio garden, creating a sense of seclusion and calm rarely found so close to central London. It provides the ideal setting for summer barbecues or quiet morning coffee. Leamington Road Villas is among Notting Hill’s most desirable addresses, within easy reach of Westbourne Grove, Golborne Road and the world-famous Portobello Market. Hyde Park and Holland Park are both within walking distance, while transport links are excellent. Westbourne Park station, served by the Hammersmith & City and Circle lines, is just four minutes away, and Notting Hill Gate for the Central line can be reached in around twenty minutes.

description: Providing exceptional value for the area it is on the very edge but not within the Canonbury Conservation Area thus enjoying protection from neighbourhood development. Built for the owners in 1998 it offers lateral living maximising the use of light & space. Finished to a high standard, this unique house is designed for modern living in uncluttered space. A privately owned road is the driveway providing ample parking (6+), the house is nestled away from prying eyes.Accommodation: large principal bedroom & en-suite bathroom, 4 further double bedrooms & family bathroom on the 1st floor. The ground floor has a 29ft triple aspect reception room accessing to 2 courtyards, dining/ family room with access to a courtyard along with kitchen, separate utility room & another bathroom.The courtyards, designed by Joe Swift, open onto the driveway via opening full side gates for further access & sense of open space. They provide both beauty & privacy being perfect for alfresco entertaining.The flow & sense of space is uniquely designed to include the courtyards via wide opening doors expanding the house into the courtyards & vice versa. This contributes to a breathtaking living experience over four aspects which is unavailable with any other property in Islington.Storage is excellent & blends in with the house’s fabric to allow a very uncluttered look & feel, a blank canvas for any owners’ design ideas.Though currently lapsed, the house had planning permission to add another floor plus glazing in one courtyard & has current planning permission for an extension on the first floor: with potential for adding 2,000sf in total (CGI Images above). Any addition will add a multiple of the cost to the value further future-proofing long term prospects.For many however, this house needs no expansion as the bespoke design reduces utility space such that it feels like a house 50% larger than its’ footprint suggests.The house is accessed via a privately owned cul de sac to the rear of a small, well-kept local gastropub, popular with neighbours & known for its community atmosphere. Its presence adds to the neighbourhood security & vibrancy without intruding on the privacy of the home.The secluded location creates a private retreat meaning no invasion of privacy & no passing traffic, leading to it being a quiet restful place to live with immediate access to the liveliness of Upper Street & excellent travel hubs when desired.Travel & communication are excellent with Underground, Overground & National & international hubs within minutes. The City & the West End are quickly accessible due to the central location.Perfect for those who value refined living in a real London neighbourhood. A rare blend of central convenience & tucked-away privacy in this extraordinary home.Selling your property in Hydes Place? Trying to find your perfect home to buy? A simply stunning double-fronted Victorian family home with a 64 ft south-facing garden and gated off-street parking on this highly sought after residential street a short distance from Wandsworth Common. Offering almost 3,600 sq ft of beautifully finished accommodation, cleverly balancing period detailing with more contemporary touches, the property is set well back from the road and benefits from gated off-street parking for two cars at the front. With double doors from the large entrance hall, there is an elegant formal reception room with high ceilings, washed oak floors and bespoke alcove cabinetry flanking a contemporary style fireplace. On the other side is a cozy, less formal drawing room with a flame effect gas fire. There is a further reception room to the rear, which could be used as a playroom, or else a useful study overlooking the beautiful, landscaped garden.The heart of the home is the spectacular kitchen, dining and family room. Glazed on two sides and lit from above by a large roof light, the room is incredibly light and airy and floor-to-ceiling sliding doors create a truly in/out space. With large format tiled floors, there is a smart, contemporary kitchen with plenty of fitted storage, integrated Gaggenau appliances and a large central island with a breakfast bar. There is ample room to comfortably seat 10 people making the space ideal for family living and for entertaining. A downstairs cloakroom completes the ground floor accommodation. There is also extensive storage, a useful room and a home gym on the lower ground floor. Six double bedrooms are arranged on the top two floors including a luxurious principal suite on the first floor with two walk-in dressing rooms and steps down to a sumptuous en-suite bathroom with twin sinks set in to a vanity, an over-sized shower with glass screen and a sunken bath under the window, perfectly framing the garden below. There are two further bedrooms on the first floor (one en-suite), together with three further double bedrooms on the top floor and a sleek contemporary family bathroom.To the rear of the house is a wonderful 64 ft south-facing garden. A paved terrace leads directly from the kitchen, ideal for al-fresco entertaining in the summer months. Steps lead up to a large Easigrass lawned area with mature borders filled with flowers, plants and shrubs. Hendham Road is an extremely well-regarded residential street running west off Trinity Road. The amenities of Bellevue Road are within easy walking distance, and the open spaces of Wandsworth Common are also nearby. Transport can be found at either Wandsworth Common which provides quick and convenient access to central London via Victoria, or at Tooting Bec Underground station providing access to the city, via Bank. A variety of bus routes run along Trinity Road. There is an excellent selection of state and private Schools in the area, which are subject to catchment and entrance each year.Council Band Tax: H | EPC: D | Tenure: FreeholdBrochuresParticulars

location: Trevor Square, Knightsbridge, London, SW7, United Kingdom
description: Set on the fifth floor of one of Knightsbridge’s most coveted addresses, this beautifully proportioned two-bedroom apartment combines light-filled interiors with a rare sense of privacy, all within moments of Harrods and the boutiques of Brompton Road. Part of the iconic redevelopment of the former Harrods Depository, 19–22 Trevor Square remains one of the area’s most sought-after residential buildings. Completed in 2002, the development has all the grandeur of a 19th-century red-brick façade complemented by chic, contemporary design, all overseen by a 24-hour concierge team. The apartment opens into a generous entrance hall, leading to a bright and expansive reception and dining room. This space is framed by full-height glazing, which draws in natural light and opens onto a private balcony, offering views across the rooftops of Knightsbridge. Pale wood floors and white walls create a calm, neutral backdrop, and there is air-conditioning and automated blinds. A fully fitted kitchen connects to both the reception room and the hallway, making it well suited to entertaining as well as everyday life. Miele and Hisense appliances are neatly integrated, while the layout allows for plenty of storage and workspace. Both bedrooms are comfortably proportioned, with bespoke built-in wardrobes and thick wool carpets underfoot. The principal suite includes an ensuite bathroom, while the second bedroom is served by a well-appointed family bathroom just off the hall. Trevor Square sits discreetly behind Brompton Road, opposite Harrods and just a short walk from Hyde Park. The area is renowned for its world-class shopping, with the designer flagships of Sloane Street and the boutiques of Beauchamp Place all close by. There is no shortage of world-famous restaurants, with neighbourhood favourites such as Zuma, LPM and Sumosan Twiga all within walking distance. Excellent transport connections and a strong sense of community make Knightsbridge as practical as it is prestigious.

description: In the heart of Mayfair, moments from Hyde Park, Culross House stands as a rare modern mansion built to an extraordinary standard. Beautifully Extended Five Bedroom Period Home with Study and Generous Garden - CHAIN FREE.This expertly extended and substantial period family home is spread generously over three and a half tasteful floors. The accommodation is presented with a slick neutral finish throughout and comprises a larger than average double reception, magnificent full-width kitchen/diner, five fantastic bedrooms (master ensuite), study, bathroom, shower room and additional guest wc. There's an impressive amount of bespoke cabinetry and a super handy storage cellar. A top floor Juliette balcony supplies sweeping panoramic views across London - great for New Year fireworks! The rear garden has been made suitably handsome and includes a sizeable paved patio, lawn and mature shrubs and plants. The location is unrivalled for its convenience. You're within the (tiny) catchment for a fantastic primary school - St John's & St Clement's C of E Primary School is highly considered and literally seconds away for the easiest morning commute! This is in addition to the wonderful amenities of Lordship Lane. Transport is a cinch with East Dulwich, Peckham Rye and Denmark Hill Stations each walkable.A solid, symmetrical period exterior leads you inward to find notably high ceilings, original cornicing and corbels. A frosted light and oblong panes in the doorway afford a bright vibe. The fantastic double reception spans over 7 metres and enjoys slick neutral styling, low-level bespoke storage, fireplace and a wide front-aspect bay window with louvered blinds on the upper and lower panes. Steps lead down from the rear to that amazing full-width kitchen/extension which boasts almost 300 sq ft of cooking, lounging, dining and entertaining space. Four separate overhead windows supply light throughout the day. There's a stunning Quartz-topped island with butler sink, Quooker tap and integrated dishwasher and bin storage. You'll also benefit from seating for four adults on the far side. A further run of cabinet and counter space on both walls ensures optimum storage and food prep opportunities. Appliances include Miele integrated hob and oven and there's plenty of space for a wine cooler and American fridge/freezer. Extra-wide pan storage and a deep pantry add to the charm. Ultra-wide sliding doors open rear to the paved patio and astro-turf lawn which is flanked on three sides by raised planters with fragrant herbs, shrubs and plants. You'll find a neat wc tucked politely under the stairs back in the hall. This also offers access to that recessed cellar. The first return supplies a generous rear-facing carpeted bedroom with fitted storage and wide bay window peering over the garden. A handy shower room completes this level. Ascending a half-flight to the first floor rewards you with a bountiful double bedroom. It's a spacious, bright room and fronts the street through a wide canted bay window. Abundant bespoke wardrobes line the far wall. Bedroom three sits next door with more bespoke storage and a rear aspect. The second return has another carpeted bedroom with garden views. It sits next to a bathroom with modern suite. On the final ascent to the second floor you pass a low-level recessed storage point on the stairwell. The study supplies the first of those spectacular views and makes the perfect work-from-home spot. Bedroom five is a fabulous space which boasts a Juliette balcony with more uninterrupted views of the Shard, the city AND Canary Wharf! There are plenty more wardrobes and additional eaves storage too. Adjoining is an ensuite wet-room style shower room with fancy contemporary suite and heated towel rail. Boho amenities of the Bellenden Road Conservation area are all at your fingertips - we love 'The Victoria Inn' and The Begging Bowl. Try the cafés, bars and restaurants or venture into East Dulwich, a 10 minute walk, for some of southeast London?s best shops. Peckham Rye Station is also around 10 minutes? walk and Denmark Hill an easy 15 minutes stroll for services to London Bridge, Victoria, Blackfriars and the fab London Overground Line. You can be at Canary Wharf (via Canada Water) in a mere 20 minutes. A whole variety of buses run into town along Peckham Road, just five minutes away. The Belham Primary free school is a two minute stroll from you door for a super easy morning commute. It's part of the same trust as the highly considered Dulwich Hamlets Schools. The popular Villa pre-prep and nursery is down the road and the Little Jungle nursery is even closer. St John's and St Clement's on Adys Road is highly considered and the Dulwich Foundation schools are just a 5 minute drive.Tenure: FreeholdCouncil Tax Band: DBrochuresFull Details

A third bathroom with a bath serves this level.The property further benefits from a lower ground floor which provides additional space for storage.LocationBalham Grove is located within the highly desirable ‘Nightingale Triangle’ a predominately residential area to the south of Nightingale Lane. Balham Grove is ideally situated close to the excellent shops, restaurants and supermarkets in Balham (0.3 miles away) and the local amenities in Clapham South (0.6 miles away) and on Bellevue Road (0.7 miles away). Transport is excellent either by underground / over ground services from Balham (0.3 miles away) or by mainline train from Wandsworth Common (0.7 miles away). There is also an excellent selection of schools in the area both in the state and private sector, including Holy Ghost and Hornsby House. Offered with no chain.The house is neutrally decorated throughout and has been well maintained during a long period of ownership. The lovely, bright ground floor is semi-open-plan providing a large and light, double living area, with high, ornate ceilings, original stripped wood floors, large windows and a pair of beautiful matching Victorian fireplaces. The wide, rear reception room spans the full width of the house and leads through an opening to the kitchen and then into a conservatory, and also separately to the garden, via French windows.The kitchen is well-fitted and sleek with fully-integrated appliances including double oven, hob and extractor, dishwasher, fridge/freezer and washer/dryer. It is mainly laid to lawn and has an extra-wide, decked side return ideal for “al-fresco” dining.Upstairs are three double bedrooms with large windows, the main bedroom having glazed doors to an en-suite shower room and large built-in wardrobes.
